source.thread:
Maya Chen: Heads-up — FinFlow onboarding: 95th p95 latency ~1.9s on prod and token cost trending 2.3x vs baseline. Renewal in ~45 days, flagging as medium risk. Thinking proactive pilot: compact INT8 swap + request-level routing to their dedicated pool for tails. Need eng + enablement to run a quick canary and an enablement session with their infra lead.

Ethan Park: can you paste the perf dash link + a sample trace?

Maya Chen: dash: https://redwood.app/d/finflow/ops?panelId=latency
Trace snippet: `req_id=ff-9001 seq=1..512 p50=80ms p95=1.9s model=redwood/llama-3-8b`

Ethan Park: thx. initial thought — try quantized variant (`llama-3-8b-int8`) with the following quick config for canary routing. Should cut tokens/sec cost by ~40% and reduce scheduling pressure.

Maya Chen: dropping config into thread. cc @Lia for enablement timing.

Ethan Park: ```
# canary routing + quantization override
{\"route_condition\": \"customer_id == \\\"finflow\\\" && weight < 0.1\",
 \"model\": \"llama-3-8b-int8\",
 \"pool\": \"dedicated-finflow-us-west\",
 \"max_concurrency\": 40,
 \"fallback\": \"redwood/llama-3-8b-raw\",
 \"monitoring\": {\"slo_p95_ms\": 1200, \"error_budget_pct\": 0.5}}
```

Lia Wong: happy to run the enablement. Recommend 60-min workshop: 15m infra walkthrough, 15m canary plan, 20m troubleshooting + 10m renewal prep. Available Wed/Thu next week.

Samira Patel: agree we should surface this in the renewal playbook. Can we get a cost delta estimate to include in the renewal capture?

Ethan Park: rough calc: current token unit cost baseline -> $0.021/token-equivalent. int8 path ~40% lower => ~ $0.0126 equivalent. For their avg monthly tokens (3.5B) that's ~ $30k/mo savings. Will refine after canary.

AutoInsights Bot: [metric alert] finflow_prod_token_rate increased 18% in 48h; avg_seq_len up 22%. Created issue #CS-1427.

Maya Chen: linking issue #CS-1427 here so eng/sre can correlate logs. @Jules SRE — can you help validate pool capacity for the canary?

Jules SRE: I can reserve a 1-GPU slot on dedicated-finflow-us-west for a 48h canary. Need IAM list and expected qps.

Ethan Park: expected qps ~120 steady, spikes 300. IAM: maya@redwood, lia@redwood, ethan@redwood. I'll push canary config once slot is reserved.

Maya Chen: perfect. I'll draft a short customer-facing note outlining the pilot benefits and savings estimate and share for review.

Samira Patel: loop me in on the renewal cadence — I'd like to mention pilot progress in the executive summary.

Maya Chen: will do. thanks everyone :thumbsup:
source._description: Proactive performance optimization pilot thread for FinFlow: propose INT8 model swap + dedicated routing canary; coordinate eng, SRE, and enablement; capture renewal impact and next steps

source.thread:
Samira Narang: Kickoff for Polaris onboarding. Quick agenda: confirm owners, define 30 day success criteria, set pilot timeline, and list early renewal risk signals. Who's in for ownership?
Ethan Park: I can own infra deployment + staging validation. need SSO details and approx expected qps.
Jules Ortiz: I'll handle product acceptance and the evaluation matrix. want to lock 5 core flows to validate.
Maya Green: CS lead here. main Polaris contacts: Nina (Head of ML), Omar (SRE), and Leah (PM). I'll intro them after this thread.
Samira Narang: Proposed success criteria: (A) 3 pilot flows running end-to-end in staging, (B) avg inference < 200ms, p95 < 400ms for their typical inputs, (C) error rate < 1% for integration tests, (D) enablement completed with recorded sessions. feedback mechanism in place.
Ethan Park: Latency note: we should separate inference-only vs end-to-end. I suggest target inference p95 < 350ms on our staging nodes, and end-to-end SLA we can set after seeing network hop times.
Jules Ortiz: Eval plan: I'll produce an eval set and baseline comparisons. also will track semantic drift and hallucination occurrences per flow.
Maya Green: Renewal risk signals to watch: missed pilot milestones, repeated auth failures, low usage in pilot, or negative qualitative feedback in first two weeks. propose weekly scorecard + escalation if any red flag.
Samira Narang: Good. Let's map owners to risks: Ethan for infra/auth, Jules for quality/regressions, Maya for adoption/engagement.
DevOpsBot: Note: created onboarding checklist https://redwood.company/polaris-onboard. Checklist owner: Samira Narang.
Ethan Park: Infra tasks: provision dedicated staging cluster, share endpoints, run baseline script. ETA: provision by Tue, baseline script results by Fri. I'll post commands in the doc.
Jules Ortiz: For feedback capture: propose in-app form (structured) + weekly consolidated doc. will add a small schema: flow_id, attempt_id, outcome, free_text.
Maya Green: Workshops: two 60m sessions 20-min demo + 40-min Q&A/office hours. Dates: Wed morning and Fri afternoon next week. I'll send invites once Nina confirms.
Samira Narang: Timeline summary: week 0 (this week) stakeholder intros + env access; week 1 infra + baselines + workshop 1; week 2 pilot runs + iterate; week 3 acceptance & recordings + ops handoff; week 4 consolidation & renewal signal check.
Ethan Park: Quick command for baseline: ```bash
curl -s -X POST $STAGING_URL -H "Authorization: Bearer $KEY" -d '{"input":"test prompt"}' | jq '.latency'
```
Jules Ortiz: I'll commit the eval prompts by Thu. also adding golden responses for comparison.
Maya Green: Action items: I'll intro Polaris team today. Samira: capture the risks + success criteria in the checklist doc. Ethan: provision staging by Tue. Jules: eval by Thu. Everyone: review onboarding doc after updates.
Samira Narang: Thanks team. I'll update the checklist and ping when the updated doc is live. :white_check_mark:

source.text:
Ava Chen: Heads up — Helios Labs wants a midterm adjustment: drop 12 seats and bill to their EU subsidiary. Procurement asks for delegated signer on DocuSign. Any compliance or finance blockers?

Noah Patel: Who's the current contract signer? Are they asking for credit or term change?

Priya Rao: Legal: delegation is OK if we get an official delegation email plus org chart. EU entity swap requires VAT/tax ID + remit address. No material T&C changes allowed. We need a short addendum referencing account id.

Liam O'Neill: Finance: need legal name, VAT, remit address. For seat drop we'll issue prorated credit. PO preferred but CC acceptable to speed up.

Ava Chen: Effective date requested 2026-06-15. They've sent a delegation pdf to procurement@redwood.com.

Marta (CS Manager): Can procurement email + doc suffice? @Priya confirm.

Priya Rao: Yes — procurement email from company domain + delegation doc acceptable. I'll draft one-page addendum now.

Noah Patel: I'll confirm delegated signer's name. If we can get CC we can close faster before PO.

Docusign Bot: Envelope 0xdef456: Sent to elena.t@helios.com for signature (1/3). Status: sent.

Ava Chen: Link to delegation doc: https://files.redwood/helios/delegation-0612.pdf

Noah Patel: Procurement confirms Elena Torres (Head of Procurement) as delegated signer. Billing requested to be "Helios EU BV".

Liam O'Neill: Please provide VAT: BE987654321 and remit address. We'll update Zuora and issue corrected invoice post-signature.

Priya Rao: Draft addendum uploaded: https://drive.redwood/helios/addendum-2026-06-15.docx. Text includes: "Seat reduction effective 2026-06-15; prorated credit to next invoice; billing entity changed to Helios EU BV; no other contract terms changed." Review pls.

Ava Chen: Pricing same per-seat?

Noah Patel: Confirmed: same per-seat rate.

Liam O'Neill: We'll calculate prorate by days in billing period (UTC). Please confirm effective date inclusive.

Priya Rao: Inserted account HEL-12345 and updated billing entity. Routing for signature now.

Docusign Bot: Envelope 0xdef456: Completed by Elena Torres. Status: completed.

Marta (CS Manager): Great. @Priya upload signed addendum to customer folder and send to billing.

Priya Rao: Uploaded and stamped 2026-06-10 counter-sign date. Forwarding to accounting.

Ava Chen: Gainsight updated, renewal risk down. Offering CC fallback if PO delays.

Noah Patel: Will log PO when received and confirm closure.

Liam O'Neill: Billing summary:
```
Account: HEL-12345
Old seats: 112
New seats: 100
Effective: 2026-06-15
Prorate: days-in-period calculation, credit to next invoice
Billing entity: Helios EU BV (VAT on file)
```

Ava Chen: Closing thread — resolved in CS tracker. Thanks everyone :white_check_mark:

source.text:
Ava Chen: Heads up — Midterm renewal ask from Helios Labs. They want to reduce 15 seats mid-contract and move billing to their EU entity. Procurement is requesting a delegated signer on our Docusign envelope. Need quick alignment on legal/finance/exec path — any red flags?

Noah Patel: Thanks. Who's the current signer on the contract? Also are they asking for prorate credit or a term adjustment?

Priya Rao: Legal: we can allow a delegated signer if we receive a written delegation (procurement email + org chart). EU entity swap requires tax ID & PO or invoice remit details. No T&C material changes ok. We'll need a short addendum referencing the account id.

Liam O'Neill: Finance: we'll need the EU billing entity legal name, VAT/tax id, and remit address. For 15-seat reduction we will issue a prorated credit effective the reduction date. If they insist on PO that will slow closure.

Ava Chen: They want effective 2026-05-01. Procurement prefers PO but can pay by CC to speed things. They've already sent a delegation PDF to procurement@redwood.com.

Marta (CS Manager): Can we accept the delegation letter via procurement email thread? If yes, let's route the envelope to the delegated signer. @Priya confirm email + doc is sufficient.

Priya Rao: Email + procurement header from company domain + delegation letter is acceptable. We still need a short signed addendum stating seat change and billing entity swap. I'll draft it now.

Noah Patel: I'll confirm the delegated signer's full name and authority. If we need an addendum, can we do a one-pager addendum with line items? I can push for CC to close before PO lands.

Docusign Bot: Envelope 0xabc123: Sent to noah.p@helios.com for signature (1/3). Status: sent.

Ava Chen: Copy of delegation letter (redacted): https://files.redwood/helios/delegation-2026-04.pdf

Noah Patel: Procurement replied — delegated signer is Elena Torres (Title: Head of Procurement). They request invoice billed to "Helios EU BV". Attached vendor confirmation.

Liam O'Neill: OK. For Helios EU BV we need VAT: BE123456789 (example), and remit address. We'll update billing system (Zuora) and issue a corrected invoice after signature.

Priya Rao: Draft addendum uploaded: https://drive.redwood/helios/addendum-seat-reduction-2026.docx. Key text: "Seat reduction effective 2026-05-01; prorated credit applied to next invoice; billing entity changed to Helios EU BV; no other contract terms altered." Please review quickly.

Ava Chen: Quick QA — pricing unchanged? They want same per-seat rate, right?

Noah Patel: Confirmed: same per-seat price, just fewer seats. If legal ok with addendum, we can route for signature.

Liam O'Neill: Finance note: we'll calculate prorate by days in the billing period. Please confirm effective date is inclusive. Also send exact timezone if they care (we'll use UTC date-only).

Priya Rao: Signed addendum template ready. I'll insert account id HEL-12345 and update the billing entity line.

Docusign Bot: Envelope 0xabc123: Signed by Elena Torres (delegated signer). Status: completed.

Marta (CS Manager): Nice work. @Priya can you upload the final signed addendum to the customer folder and forward to accounting for invoice update?

Priya Rao: On it — uploading now and will stamp counter-signed date.

Ava Chen: Helios confirmed onboarding checklist updated and seat counts adjusted. Updating Gainsight and renewal forecast now — risk decreases. Thanks everyone :tada:

Noah Patel: I'll log the PO number when it comes through and notify finance. Closing loop.

Liam O'Neill: Billing snippet for records:
```
Account: HEL-12345
Old seats: 200
New seats: 185
Effective: 2026-05-01
Prorate: calculated by days, credit applied to next invoice
Billing entity: Helios EU BV (VAT on file)
```

Ava Chen: Closing thread — marking as resolved in CS tracker. If PO stalls, we'll offer temporary CC. :white_check_mark:

source.text:
Ethan Park (Orbit): Hey CS, onboarding hiccup. After ~75s our service started seeing a 403 followed by a burst of 429s. Logs say 'token_invalid' then 'quota_exceeded: per_key'. Demo in 48h, can we triage?
Priya Khatri: Thanks Ethan. Which SDK/version and are you routing through a proxy or direct to API?
Ethan Park (Orbit): Node SDK v2.2.0 behind our API gateway. We mint short tokens TTL=120s, rotate 25s before expiry, and keep prior token for 50s so streams don't drop.
Jordan Ruiz: Keeping the previous token for 50s is likely the culprit if your gateway multiplexes connections. Two distinct tokens hitting auth in quick succession can count twice against the per-key quota.
Ethan Park (Orbit): Repro log snippet: 
# t=0 -> 200 OK
# t=80 token swap -> 403 {"error":"token_invalid"}
# immediate retries -> 429 {"error":"quota_exceeded","quota":"per_key"}
Jordan Ruiz: Two things to try: 1) reduce old-token retention to <=5s for a quick validation, 2) increase retry backoff to 150-250ms to avoid tight retry bursts.
Priya Khatri: We can also temporarily relax per-key window for Orbit while you validate. If the quick test passes we'll update onboarding docs to call out header-buffering behavior behind multiplexing proxies.
CS Bot: Ticket created: CS-9123  labels: onboarding, auth, rate-quota. Temp relax enabled until 3288884999.
Ethan Park (Orbit): Will try old-token keep=3s + backoff=200ms now, will report back in ~15m. Thanks.
Jordan Ruiz: If you use long streaming, prefer using the same token for stream lifetime and only rotate on new connections to avoid mid-stream swaps.
Priya Khatri: Follow-up: if the test is green we'll push a short playbook entry and a reproduce script for customers.
Ethan Park (Orbit): Quick test success  10m no 403/429 under ramp. Demo looks safe for now. Will confirm after wider ramp.
Priya Khatri: Great. Action items: Ethan to continue ramp and confirm, CS to add playbook note, eng to attach logs to CS-9123 and close when stable. Thanks all. :tada:

Priya Khatri: We can also temporarily relax per-key window for Orbit while you validate. If the quick test passes we'll update onboarding docs to call out header-buffering behavior behind multiplexing proxies.

Ethan Park (Orbit): Will try old-token keep=3s + backoff=200ms now, will report back in ~15m. Thanks.
Jordan Ruiz: If you use long streaming, prefer using the same token for stream lifetime and only rotate on new connections to avoid mid-stream swaps.

source.messages:
incident-bot: ALERT: High error rate detected on /v1/generate for dedicated-cluster-5. Error rate spikes started ~2026-03-12T02:28Z. Pager on-call: @Samir. Automated mitigation: routing fallback applied to tier-2 models.
Samir Patel: Ack, joining bridge now. setting priority=high. will post brief status page copy for Maya to push.
Maya Lopez: thanks Samir — CS team needs: 1) short status-page blurb, 2) customer email draft (single para + short timeline), 3) confirmation on data exposure.
Samir Patel: quick update: rolled back last routing policy change (deploy id: route-2026-03-12-02). Fallback to model-variant b1 reduced errors from ~18% -> ~1.2% in 6m. Still seeing intermittent 429s for few tenants on cluster-5. Investigating root cause (suspect compile/kernel selection mismatch after that route change).
Ben Carter: saw surge in compile backlog on g4-pool around 02:26Z. log snippet: ```ERROR compile_queue_timeout model=redwood-opus-v2 shard=3 duration_ms=5020``` Backpressure propagated and caused generator to return 503/429.
Samir Patel: proposed status page blurb below — Maya feel free to shorten. Also adding a CS email draft after that.
Samir Patel: STATUS PAGE (proposed): 
- "Incident: Increased error rates for generation requests to dedicated-cluster-5. Mitigation: traffic routed to fallback model variants; errors substantially reduced. Impact: some requests may have experienced 5xx/429 responses between 02:25–02:40 UTC. Current status: degraded (mitigation in place)."
Samir Patel: CUSTOMER EMAIL DRAFT (proposed): 
"Hi Eli — we experienced increased errors affecting generation requests on your dedicated cluster for a ~15 minute window today (2026-03-12 02:25–02:40 UTC). We routed traffic to fallback model variants which reduced failed calls and restored normal service. We do not believe any customer data was exposed. We will share a full timeline, root cause analysis, and proposed preventative actions within 48 hours. Apologies for the disruption — Jordan will follow up with credit details and next steps.
— Samir & Redwood Support"
Jordan (CSM): draft looks good. For BrightApps can we state tentative credit: 10% of the invoice for the affected hour(s) or equivalent $ credit? Also add line: "If you'd like, we can schedule a 30-min call to walk the timeline and mitigation."
Maya Lopez: also add a one-line exec summary at top: "Service briefly degraded for a subset of dedicated customers; mitigation applied."
Eli (BrightApps): thanks — appreciate the quick mitigation. Two Qs: 1) timeline please, 2) was any user data or logs sent to 3rd-party?
Samir Patel: timeline (preliminary):
- 02:24: change route-2026-03-12-02 deployed to routing plane
- 02:25: compile backlog spike observed on g4-pool -> increased compile latency
- 02:26: first 5xx/429 observed for some tenants on cluster-5
- 02:31: automated routing fallback triggered, some traffic shifted to b1 variants
- 02:34: manual rollback of route-2026-03-12-02 completed (traffic restored to previous stable route)
- 02:40: error rate returned to baseline (~0.1%)
Samir Patel: on data exposure: we ran quick grep and audit of egress paths and 3rd-party integrations for affected instances — no evidence of data leaving Redwood boundaries. command used: `rg "upload_to_s3|external_callback" /var/logs/cluster-5 -n --no-ignore-vcs` returned no matches for the affected timeframe.
Ben Carter: remediation actions for post-incident roadmap: 1) add circuit-breaker on routing changes to prevent wide fanout of unvalidated rules, 2) increase compile workers for g4-pool during deploy windows, 3) add tighter compile-time SLO and early detection alerts.
Samir Patel: agreed. I'll capture these in the RCA doc. Action items:
- Samir: produce full RCA draft + prevention plan within 48h (assigned)
- Ben: run capacity check and propose autoscale tweak for g4-pool by EOD + add test for compile backlog alerts
- Jordan: prepare customer credit proposal for BrightApps (suggest 10% credit for impacted minute-window) and schedule follow-up call if Eli wants
- Maya: update status page with approved short blurb and link to follow-up RCA when published
Jordan (CSM): Eli — we propose a 10% credit for the impacted hour prorated across your usage that window. If that sounds OK we'll process it and include in the next billing cycle. Want to set a 30-min call Wednesday 10:00 UTC?
Eli (BrightApps): 30-min Wed 10:00 UTC works. Credit proposal sounds fair. Also please confirm whether the fallback model affected tokenization/outputs for ongoing prompts — any risk of silent semantic changes?
Samir Patel: fallback model b1 is a compatible variant with same tokenizer; we ran spot checks on customer prompts and found equivalent outputs within acceptable variance. No silent truncation or PII leaks discovered.
Jordan (CSM): noted — scheduling call and will send calendar invite. We'll send a short customer-facing timeline + credit confirmation after Samir posts the RCA draft.
Maya Lopez: thanks team. Closing notes: keep thread for RCA link + final status page URL. :thumbsup:
Samir Patel: will update thread with RCA link and final mitigation checklist when ready.
Jordan (CSM): thanks everyone.

source.thread:
Rhea Kapoor (CS): quick note  Orchid Legal's EU pilot kept failing when partners ran multi-tenant demos. their outbound is NAT'd via two small prefixes. They're requesting an allowlist + short throughput lift. renewal check-in in 6 days, flagging as at-risk until resolved.
Ben Carter (SE): can you post the CIDRs and desired qps/duration? Any strict data residency needs?
Rhea Kapoor (CS): CIDRs: 198.51.100.64/28 and 203.0.113.32/29. Region preference: eu-central-1 (Frankfurt). Request: 25 -> 100 QPS for 48h around the demo window. Data residency: must keep logs+payload in EU.
Ivy Zhou (Onboard): they hit burst protection mid-demo and partners dropped the call. If we don't stabilize performance they'll push renewal to competitive eval. can we set autorevoke and a 24h pre-expiry reminder?
Harish Patel (NetEng): We can add the prefixes to the EU edge allowlist and pin routing to eu-central-1. No maint window required. I'll tag rules with expiry and `autorevoke:true`.
Ben Carter (SE): also will add a project routing override and bias KV/cache locality to Frankfurt. set a soft safety cap at 140 QPS in case of unexpected spikes.
Rhea Kapoor (CS): ops payload (expiry RFC3339):
```
allowlist:
  - cidr: "198.51.100.64/28"
    note: "orchid-legal demo temp"
  - cidr: "203.0.113.32/29"
    note: "orchid-legal demo temp"
allowlist_expires: "2026-05-10T12:00:00Z"
rate_limits:
  project_id: "orchid-legal-prod"
  qps: "100"
  duration: "48h"
region_pin: "eu-central-1"
soft_limit: "140"
```
Ivy Zhou (Onboard): please also add a Slack reminder 24h before expiry and a CRM flag "escalation-mitigated" if demo completes successfully.
Harish Patel (NetEng): Applying change now. Will run a smoke test from eu-central edge.
ops-bot: Ticket CS-6120 created  "Orchid Legal: eu-central allowlist + 48h QPS lift". Assignees: Harish Patel, Ben Carter. ETA 20m.
Harish Patel (NetEng): applied and verified via curl simulation. firewall logs show accept from both CIDRs. revocation job queued. :white_check_mark:
ops-bot: CS-6120 status -> done. reminder scheduled for 2026-05-09T12:00:00Z.
Ivy Zhou (Onboard): customer reported demo success and noticed improved P95 latency. Marked as escalation mitigated in CRM.
Rhea Kapoor (CS): great work team  I'll log a short post-demo survey to capture partner feedback and update renewal notes. :thumbsup:

source.text:
Elena Ward: Quick batch update — pulled 20 in-product survey rows (NPS followups), 14 voice snippets from onboarding, and 6 support threads. Aim: surface top 8 distinct customer asks (atomic), tag product/enablement/infra, and mark any "renewal_cohort" customers for rapid escalation. Need a tidy CSV + 1-slide summary for PM sync tomorrow.

Marcus Lin: I can run the first pass. Plan: merge telemetry (event_id) + ASR transcripts + support excerpts, run sentiment + NLU extraction, then human-validate low-confidence items. Will output one row per atomic ask.

Sophie Turner: Love the telemetry tie-in. For scoring, let's compute two scores:
- signal_score = normalized frequency * 2 + (sentiment_neg ? 3 : 0) + (explicit_request ? 2 : 0)
- churn_alert = renewal_cohort ? 1 : 0 (and escalate if signal_score >=7 || churn_alert==1)
Also add 'evidence_refs' column with event_ids/clip_ids/ticket_ids.

Jorge Alvarez: CSV header suggestion (minimal):
```
customer,source,ref_id,ask,category,signal_score,churn_alert,sentiment,enablement_ok,evidence_refs,notes
```

signal-synth-bot: Uploaded merged bundle: inprod_survey_voice_3135792468.zip -> https://drive.redwood.example/cs/bundles/inprod_survey_voice_3135792468.zip :file_folder:

Marcus Lin: Ran NLU + sentiment. Sample rows (first-pass):
NimbusHealth,inproduct,event_9921,"Per-route cost toggle in Console","billing",8,1,negative,false,"event_9921,clip_nim_03","explicit during renewal survey"
ParcelCo,voice,pc_clip_07,"Clarify token billing for batch embeddings","billing",5,0,neutral,true,"pc_clip_07,ticket_pc_402","enablement could help"
Veridian,support,vd-77,"Private model pinning required for compliance","infra",9,1,negative,false,"vd-77,clip_vd_01","security lead requested on-call"

Sophie Turner: Nice. For dedupe: group by fuzzy-match on 'ask' text + same pattern across sources -> produce canonical pattern_id. If cluster_size >=3 create pattern ticket for PMs with all evidence refs.

Elena Ward: Good call on pattern tickets. Also, add a 'sampling_bias' note if the ask source is mostly surveys (we don't want to overweight surveys vs support calls).

Marcus Lin: Noted — I'll include a confidence label (high/med/low) and a sampling_bias field in notes. Also flagged 2 survey responses that looked like 'noise' (one-liners) -> confidence=low.

Jorge Alvarez: Automation rules proposal:
- If signal_score >= 7 OR churn_alert==1 -> auto-open 'customer-voice-escalation' ticket and ping the CSM + account exec.
- If enablement_ok==true and signal_score <7 -> create a docs task in 'cs-enablement'.
- If pattern cluster_size >=3 -> create a pattern-card in PM backlog for grouping.

Sophie Turner: For auto-tickets include: short ask, top 2 evidence refs, sentiment excerpt, suggested owner_reco (product/enablement/infra), and estimated_effort (S/M/L).

Marcus Lin: Uploaded CSV: inprod_voice_extract_3135792468.csv -> https://drive.redwood.example/cs/exports/inprod_voice_extract_3135792468.csv. I flagged 3 low-audio clips as 'needs-listen' and 1 row as 'needs-legal' (customer quoted PII in transcript).

Elena Ward: Great. Jorge please own the PM-facing one-pager (top 5 product asks, top 3 escalations with renewal context, and pattern heatmap). Sophie will own dedupe/cluster IDs. Marcus will wire the auto-ticket preview for review.

signal-synth-bot: Reminder: CS->Product triage tomorrow 09:30. Agenda auto-added: "In-product survey + voice harvest 3135792468: top patterns & churn alerts".

Sophie Turner: Quick question — for 'renewal_cohort' logic, should we include customers with upcoming contract renegotiation in 90 days or only those flagged by CSMs?

Elena Ward: Include both. We tag 'renewal_cohort' if either CSM flagged or contract_end <= 90d. We should escalate if churn_alert==1 even if signal_score is marginal.

Marcus Lin: Understood. I'll push final CSV with pattern_id column and the auto-ticket preview before EOD.

Jorge Alvarez: Thanks team. If anyone needs access to the bundle, DM me. See you in the triage. :thumbsup:

text:
Elena Ward: Quick batch update — pulled 20 in-product survey rows (NPS followups), 14 voice snippets from onboarding, and 6 support threads. Aim: surface top 8 distinct customer asks (atomic), tag product/enablement/infra, and mark any "renewal_cohort" customers for rapid escalation. Need a tidy CSV + 1-slide summary for PM sync tomorrow.

Marcus Lin: I can run the first pass. Plan: merge telemetry (event_id) + ASR transcripts + support excerpts, run sentiment + NLU extraction, then human-validate low-confidence items. Will output one row per atomic ask.

Sophie Turner: Love the telemetry tie-in. For scoring, let's compute two scores:
- signal_score = normalized frequency * 2 + (sentiment_neg ? 3 : 0) + (explicit_request ? 2 : 0)
- churn_alert = renewal_cohort ? 1 : 0 (and escalate if signal_score >=7 || churn_alert==1)
Also add 'evidence_refs' column with event_ids/clip_ids/ticket_ids.

Jorge Alvarez: CSV header suggestion (minimal):
```
customer,source,ref_id,ask,category,signal_score,churn_alert,sentiment,enablement_ok,evidence_refs,notes
```

signal-synth-bot: Uploaded merged bundle: inprod_survey_voice_3135792468.zip -> https://drive.redwood.example/cs/bundles/inprod_survey_voice_3135792468.zip :file_folder:

Marcus Lin: Ran NLU + sentiment. Sample rows (first-pass):
NimbusHealth,inproduct,event_9921,\"Per-route cost toggle in Console\",\"billing\",8,1,negative,false,\"event_9921,clip_nim_03\",\"explicit during renewal survey\"
ParcelCo,voice,pc_clip_07,\"Clarify token billing for batch embeddings\",\"billing\",5,0,neutral,true,\"pc_clip_07,ticket_pc_402\",\"enablement could help\"
Veridian,support,vd-77,\"Private model pinning required for compliance\",\"infra\",9,1,negative,false,\"vd-77,clip_vd_01\",\"security lead requested on-call\"

Sophie Turner: Nice. For dedupe: group by fuzzy-match on 'ask' text + same pattern across sources -> produce canonical pattern_id. If cluster_size >=3 create pattern ticket for PMs with all evidence refs.

Elena Ward: Good call on pattern tickets. Also, add a 'sampling_bias' note if the ask source is mostly surveys (we don't want to overweight surveys vs support calls).

Marcus Lin: Noted — I'll include a confidence label (high/med/low) and a sampling_bias field in notes. Also flagged 2 survey responses that looked like 'noise' (one-liners) -> confidence=low.

Jorge Alvarez: Automation rules proposal:
- If signal_score >= 7 OR churn_alert==1 -> auto-open 'customer-voice-escalation' ticket and ping the CSM + account exec.
- If enablement_ok==true and signal_score <7 -> create a docs task in 'cs-enablement'.
- If pattern cluster_size >=3 -> create a pattern-card in PM backlog for grouping.

Sophie Turner: For auto-tickets include: short ask, top 2 evidence refs, sentiment excerpt, suggested owner_reco (product/enablement/infra), and estimated_effort (S/M/L).

Marcus Lin: Uploaded CSV: inprod_voice_extract_3135792468.csv -> https://drive.redwood.example/cs/exports/inprod_voice_extract_3135792468.csv. I flagged 3 low-audio clips as 'needs-listen' and 1 row as 'needs-legal' (customer quoted PII in transcript).

Elena Ward: Great. Jorge please own the PM-facing one-pager (top 5 product asks, top 3 escalations with renewal context, and pattern heatmap). Sophie will own dedupe/cluster IDs. Marcus will wire the auto-ticket preview for review.

signal-synth-bot: Reminder: CS->Product triage tomorrow 09:30. Agenda auto-added: \"In-product survey + voice harvest 3135792468: top patterns & churn alerts\".

Sophie Turner: Quick question — for 'renewal_cohort' logic, should we include customers with upcoming contract renegotiation in 90 days or only those flagged by CSMs?

Elena Ward: Include both. We tag 'renewal_cohort' if either CSM flagged or contract_end <= 90d. We should escalate if churn_alert==1 even if signal_score is marginal.

Marcus Lin: Understood. I'll push final CSV with pattern_id column and the auto-ticket preview before EOD.

Jorge Alvarez: Thanks team. If anyone needs access to the bundle, DM me. See you in the triage. :thumbsup:

source.content:
Ava Chen: Quick heads-up: I'm running a legacy CRM -> playbook sync dry-run tomorrow 08:00 PT. Dry run only, no live webhooks. Goal: validate mapping, dedupe hooks, healthscore recalcs.
Miguel Torres: Scope? full records or trimmed?
Ava Chen: Full contact + account records, skip attachments/custom objects. Will sample 10k. Outputs to staging S3.
Riley Patel: I'll validate bucket thresholds and highlight big movers. Please include raw and recalculated score columns.
Ava Chen: Doing --sample 10000, dump to s3://redwood-csops/dryruns/3112345678/
Jordan Lee: Ensure playbook automation is disabled so no emails fire.
Ava Chen: Correct: dry_run=true, notifications to #csops-dry-run only.
Sam Park: Renewal asked to see accounts that move from low->medium. Can we tag those with cs-dry-preview?
Ava Chen: Adding cs-dry-preview and will include top-5 signals per account in the preview CSV.
playbook-bot: Scheduled dry-run job created by @Ava Chen  job-id job-3112345678  ETA 08:00 PT  mode: dry_run
Ava Chen: mapping snippet (staging):
account.owner_id -> contact.account_owner_id
crm.renewal_probability -> health.renewal_risk_score
last_touch.ts -> telemetry.last_success_touch
legacy.tags -> health.tags (append only)
Miguel Torres: Note legacy multiplied renewal_probability by 0.9 for accounts older than 24 months. Not porting unless product signs off.
Ava Chen: I'll set apply_lifecycle_adjustment=false for this run and surface diffs.
Riley Patel: Test plan: 1) run sample, 2) compare old vs new buckets, 3) list accounts moving >1 bucket and top signals. I'll own step 3.
Jordan Lee: Post-run check: owner consistency script to ensure no account ends with multiple owners. I'll run it.
Ava Chen: Post-run checklist commands:
python tools/check_owner_consistency.py --run-id job-3112345678
python tools/generate_renewal_preview.py --run-id job-3112345678 --out s3://redwood-csops/dryruns/3112345678/renewal_preview.csv
playbook-bot: job-3112345678 started at 08:00:03 PT  dry_run=true  sample=10000
Ava Chen: Run complete. Artifacts: s3://redwood-csops/dryruns/3112345678/health_samples.csv, renewal_preview.csv, run_notes.md. Summary: 10k processed, 387 accounts moved up one bucket, 22 down one bucket, 4 owner changes flagged (likely from prior duplicate merges).
Riley Patel: Quick check: ~260 of the ups were from corrected last_touch values. Will produce a short doc with examples.
Jordan Lee: Owner script: 0 accounts with >1 owner, 4 owner changes recorded (see run_notes).
Sam Park: Renewal preview filtered by cs-dry-preview sent to renewal team; they've promised EOD async review.
Miguel Torres: I'll open a follow-up about lifecycle_adjustment rule.
Ava Chen: Action items: Miguel -> PR for lifecycle rule; Riley -> healthscore diff doc; Jordan -> add owner_consistency to runbook; Sam -> coordinate renewal review. I'll file the dry-run report and schedule live sync after signoffs. Thanks all. :tada:

Ava Chen: Heads-up — I'm going to run a dry-run of the legacy CRM -> new playbook sync tomorrow 08:00 PT. Goal: validate mapping, dedupe hooks, healthscore recalcs. Will not flip live webhooks unless someone says go. :eyes:
Miguel Torres: thanks, what's the exact scope? fields + tags or full contact/account copy?
Ava Chen: full contact + account, but we'll skip attachments and custom objects. Primary things to validate: owner mapping, renewal_risk tag, last_success_touch timestamp, health_score buckets. I'll paste the mapping snippet in a sec.
Riley Patel: I'll validate healthscore thresholds once you run it. Can you output a sample of recalculated scores (10k sample) to staging S3?
Ava Chen: yep — planning to run with --sample 10000 and dump to s3://redwood-csops/dryruns/3111111111/health_samples.csv
Jordan Lee: confirm the playbook automation is on safe mode? we don't want a cluster of follow-up emails firing.
Ava Chen: playbook automation flag will be OFF (dry_run=true). Also will route notifications to #csops-dry-run channel only.
Sam Park: heads-up — renewal team asked to get a peek at any accounts that move from "low" to "medium" risk. Can we tag them with cs-dry-preview so they can filter?
Ava Chen: yes adding tag cs-dry-preview. Will include sample export for renewal (csv + top 5 signals per account).
Miguel Torres: dedupe hook fixed in staging last night (PR #422). I ran a smoke test, duplicate account merge didn't reassign owner incorrectly.
playbook-bot: Scheduled dry-run job created by @Ava Chen — job id job-3111111111 — ETA 08:00 PT — mode: dry_run — notify: #csops-dry-run
Ava Chen: mapping snippet (staging):
```
account.owner_id -> contact.account_owner_id
crm.renewal_probability -> health.renewal_risk_score
last_touch.ts -> telemetry.last_success_touch
legacy.tags -> health.tags (append only, no overwrite)
```
Miguel Torres: note — legacy had a rule where renewal_probability was multiplied by 0.9 for accounts older than 24 months. We shouldn't carry that over unless product signs off.
Ava Chen: good catch. I'll set that as a flag (apply_lifecycle_adjustment=false) for this run. We'll surface the diff.
Riley Patel: healthscore test plan: 1) run sample, 2) bucketize by old and new algorithm, 3) highlight accounts moving >1 bucket, 4) surface top contributing signals. I'll own step 3.
Jordan Lee: add a quick dedupe sanity check: ensure no account gets >1 owner after merge and the account_id remains stable. I can run a small script post-run.
Ava Chen: agreed. I'll add a post-checklist with scripts:
```
# post-run checks
python tools/check_owner_consistency.py --run-id job-3111111111
python tools/generate_renewal_preview.py --run-id job-3111111111 --out s3://redwood-csops/dryruns/3111111111/renewal_preview.csv
```
Sam Park: do we have a way to capture CS feedback quickly? maybe a short form that feeds into the playbook QA board?
Ava Chen: I'll create a short Google Form and attach it to the sample export. Link will be in the run notes. Also adding a Slack thread template for quick thumbs-up / issues.
Miguel Torres: any retention window concerns for the sample export? Staging S3 lifecycle is 30 days by default. ok?
Ava Chen: 30 days is fine for this. We'll also paste a small CSV (1k rows) in the thread for quick eyeballs.
playbook-bot: job-3111111111 started at 08:00:05 PT — dry_run=true — sample=10000 — expected duration ~ 18m
Ava Chen: running now. tagging @Riley @Jordan @Miguel for quick checks during run.
Riley Patel: watching. will comment on big movers within 30 mins. :thumbsup:
Jordan Lee: on it. will kick off owner_consistency script as soon as job finishes.
Miguel Torres: will scan for duplicate merges and owner flips.
Ava Chen: run finished. artifacts: s3://redwood-csops/dryruns/3111111111/health_samples.csv, renewal_preview.csv, run_notes.md. Summary: 10k sample processed, 412 accounts moved up one health bucket, 28 moved down one bucket, 3 potential owner flips detected but all attributable to prior duplicate merges (see notes).
Riley Patel: quick read — of the 412 moved up, 290 were reclassified due to cleaned last_touch timestamps (legacy had stale zeros). Good result.
Jordan Lee: owner_consistency script output: 0 accounts with >1 owner, 3 owner changes flagged (matching Ava's note).
Sam Park: renewal preview looks good. I filtered cs-dry-preview tag and sent the CSV to renewal team. they asked for a short async review by EOD.
Ava Chen: thanks team. Action items: Miguel to open follow-up to decide lifecycle_adjustment rule (PR suggestion). Riley to prepare a short doc on healthscore diffs + examples. Jordan to add the owner_consistency check to the regular runbook. Sam to coordinate renewal team's review. I'll file the dry-run report and close job-3111111111.
Miguel Torres: filing PR draft now.
Riley Patel: starting doc.
Jordan Lee: adding check to runbook.
Sam Park: ping renewal team for review.
Ava Chen: closing thread — will schedule full live sync after signoffs. Thanks all. :tada:

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Quick heads-up — onboarding call with Lumenix tomorrow 10am ET. They specifically asked for SLO evidence and an updated latency-credit calc before renewal review. We promised a short bundle: dashboard screenshots, p95/p50 export, and an incident timeline. Can we assemble a minimal packet by EOD? cc @Marcus Patel @Sophie Rivera

Marcus Patel: On it. I can export per-route metrics and a CSV. Which routes should we prioritize? I assume `/v1/generate` and `/v1/embeddings` — that's what they call from prod.

Sophie Rivera: Yep, prioritize those two. I'll draft a one-page explainer on how we compute credits (lookback window, exclusion windows, rounding). Plan to call out the SLOs we promised: 99.95% uptime and 200ms p95 for short prompts (<=256 tokens). Anyone seen notable variance in the last 30 days?

Jake Morales: We had a brief autoscale cold-start event last Tues that hit p95 ~600ms for ~7m on dedicated nodes. I can paste the relevant logs and the timeline. Logs (snippet):
```
[2026-02-17T14:12:30Z] scale-event: cold_start pool-A node-17
[2026-02-17T14:14:05Z] recovery: pool-A node-17 ready
errors: transient queue-depth spike -> +0.8s latency
```

Marcus Patel: Thanks Jake. I'll pull the raw numbers and include the sample-level percentiles. From quick look: last 30d p95 /v1/generate = 218ms, p50 = 58ms, p99 = 720ms (the p99 skew is from that one incident). I'll attach a link to the per-route dashboard and export. https://redwood.internal/dashboards/slo/lumenix (internal) :chart_with_upwards_trend: 

OncallBot: incident #INC-2026-472 created for autoscale cold-start spike (assigned: Jake Morales) :rotating_light:

CS Ops: I can run the SLO-verify harness against a sample prompt set to reproduce latency distribution. Need a sanitized prompt set + API key for their dedicated environment. If we can't get the key, I can run against staging and annotate differences.

Ava Chen: Good plan. Sophie — can you draft the single-page credit calc with an example? Marcus — export CSVs for /v1/generate and /v1/embeddings (last 30d + last 7d). Jake — paste the incident timeline and confirm whether that cold-start should be considered an exclusion under our policy. CS Ops — run SLO-verify and share a short runbook we can send the customer.

Sophie Rivera: Drafting now. I'll include an example calc showing a 0.03% deviation -> X credits, plus text explaining the exclusion rules (maintenance window vs infrastructure cold-starts). Will ping for review when done. :memo: 

Marcus Patel: CSV exports queued — will drop in the thread when ready. Also will add a couple of screenshots from the per-route latency heatmap.

Jake Morales: Timeline pasted into Gist: https://gist.github.com/redwood/abcd1234 — incident root cause: autoscale cold-start; mitigation: pre-warming policy rollout planned for dedicated pools. I recommend classifying this as operational (not scheduled maintenance) — need CS to decide if it qualifies for credit exclusion.

Ava Chen: Decision for the packet: include the incident as part of the timeline, note mitigation (pre-warming), but mark it as billable unless legal/escalation says otherwise. We'll present both sides to Lumenix. Goal: finalize bundle and send by 6pm PT today so it can be reviewed before the call. Thanks all. :pray: 

CS Ops: Confirmed — I'll run SLO-verify now and report results in ~45min. If anyone has a sanitized prompt set sooner, DM me.

Sophie Rivera: Draft complete, dropping in thread in 30.

Marcus Patel: CSVs and screenshots uploaded to the shared folder: https://drive.redwood.com/s/lumenix-slo-evidence (internal).

Ava Chen: Excellent. If nothing unexpected, we'll ship the packet at 18:00 PT. I'll own the customer note and call deck.

Jake Morales: Pre-warm rollout scheduled for next week, I'll include the rollout plan in the bundle.

Ava Chen: Thanks team — big win if we can close this before the renewal review.

source.text:
Ava Chen: FYI I pushed a small script to sync the onboarding asset checklist across playbooks. Noticed a handful of accounts missing the `welcome_call` step in HubSpot -> playbook mapping, which blocks our automated enablement emails. Rolling it out as a dry-run in staging tonight. Ping me if this would interfere with any ongoing backfills :wave:

Tom Ruiz: Thanks Ava — where's the repo/PR? want to skim before staging run. Also curious if this touches healthscore fields or just playbook metadata.

Ava Chen: PR here: https://github.com/redwood-inference/cs-ops/PR/482 (branch: cs/onboard-check-sync). It's additive — creates `onboard_assets_missing` boolean and appends missing steps to the playbook metadata. Does NOT change the computed healthscore.

Maya Patel: Phew ok. If it's only metadata we can still have playbooks pick it up for the next run. Can we do a backfill for all accounts created in the last 90 days after the staging verification? Some churned trials are impacted.

Ava Chen: Yep plan is: 1) run on staging with `--dry-run` to produce a CSV; 2) review sample of 100 accounts; 3) if looks good, run a one-off backfill in prod over 90d window. Backfill command: `python tools/sync_onboard_assets.py --window 90 --apply`

Jonah Klein: Can you paste the SQL you used to detect missing steps? want to ensure no joins that explode.

Ava Chen: SQL used to find gaps — lightweight:
```sql
SELECT a.account_id, p.playbook_id, s.step_key
FROM accounts a
LEFT JOIN playbook_steps p ON p.playbook_id = a.playbook_id
LEFT JOIN account_steps s ON s.account_id = a.account_id AND s.step_key = p.step_key
WHERE p.required = true AND s.step_key IS NULL
LIMIT 100;
```
Should be fine for incremental runs; we batch per playbook to keep queries small.

Tom Ruiz: Cool. Two asks: 1) add a `dry_run_sample` flag so we can easily rerun with a deterministic seed; 2) emit a small audit event for each change so CS can review in the console later.

Ava Chen: Good calls — added `--seed` and I emit `cs.onboard.asset_sync` events to `cs-audit` topic. Events include account_id, before_steps, after_steps, actor.

Maya Patel: About risk scoring — can we have an opt-in toggle so accounts flagged as "missing_welcome_call" don't change renewal risk until cs-review? We don't want false positives in churn alerts.

Ava Chen: Yes, default is opt-out (no risk changes). I added an optional `apply_risk_tag=true` flag for the backfill, but it's off by default. We'll coordinate with risk team before turning it on.

Jonah Klein: Sounds good. I'll schedule a staging dry-run tomorrow 09:30 PT and invite the risk / enablement folks to review the sample CSV. Please push any small fixes today so I can include them.

CS Ops Bot: deploybot: Rolling deployment for `cs/onboard-check-sync` to staging started by Ava Chen — build #842 queued. :rocket:

Ava Chen: Thanks — I'll post the dry-run CSV to #customer-success when complete and attach the sampled accounts. If anyone wants a pre-review of the CSV format DM me. :memo:

Tom Ruiz: Cool. Two asks: 1) add a `dry_run_sample` flag so we can easily rerun with a deterministic seed; 2) emit a small audit event for each change so CS can review in the console later.

Maya Patel: About risk scoring — can we have an opt-in toggle so accounts flagged as "missing_welcome_call" don't change renewal risk until cs-review? We don't want false positives in churn alerts.

Jonah Klein: Sounds good. I'll schedule a staging dry-run tomorrow 09:30 PT and invite the risk / enablement folks to review the sample CSV. Please push any small fixes today so I can include them.

text:
Ava Chen: FYI I pushed a small script to sync the onboarding asset checklist across playbooks. Noticed a handful of accounts missing the `welcome_call` step in HubSpot -> playbook mapping, which blocks our automated enablement emails. Rolling it out as a dry-run in staging tonight. Ping me if this would interfere with any ongoing backfills :wave:

Tom Ruiz: Thanks Ava — where's the repo/PR? want to skim before staging run. Also curious if this touches healthscore fields or just playbook metadata.

Ava Chen: PR here: https://github.com/redwood-inference/cs-ops/PR/482 (branch: cs/onboard-check-sync). It's additive — creates `onboard_assets_missing` boolean and appends missing steps to the playbook metadata. Does NOT change the computed healthscore.

Maya Patel: Phew ok. If it's only metadata we can still have playbooks pick it up for the next run. Can we do a backfill for all accounts created in the last 90 days after the staging verification? Some churned trials are impacted.

Ava Chen: Yep plan is: 1) run on staging with `--dry-run` to produce a CSV; 2) review sample of 100 accounts; 3) if looks good, run a one-off backfill in prod over 90d window. Backfill command: `python tools/sync_onboard_assets.py --window 90 --apply`

Jonah Klein: Can you paste the SQL you used to detect missing steps? want to ensure no joins that explode.

Ava Chen: SQL used to find gaps — lightweight:
```sql
SELECT a.account_id, p.playbook_id, s.step_key
FROM accounts a
LEFT JOIN playbook_steps p ON p.playbook_id = a.playbook_id
LEFT JOIN account_steps s ON s.account_id = a.account_id AND s.step_key = p.step_key
WHERE p.required = true AND s.step_key IS NULL
LIMIT 100;
```
Should be fine for incremental runs; we batch per playbook to keep queries small.

Tom Ruiz: Cool. Two asks: 1) add a `dry_run_sample` flag so we can easily rerun with a deterministic seed; 2) emit a small audit event for each change so CS can review in the console later.

Ava Chen: Good calls — added `--seed` and I emit `cs.onboard.asset_sync` events to `cs-audit` topic. Events include account_id, before_steps, after_steps, actor.

Maya Patel: About risk scoring — can we have an opt-in toggle so accounts flagged as "missing_welcome_call" don't change renewal risk until cs-review? We don't want false positives in churn alerts.

Ava Chen: Yes, default is opt-out (no risk changes). I added an optional `apply_risk_tag=true` flag for the backfill, but it's off by default. We'll coordinate with risk team before turning it on.

Jonah Klein: Sounds good. I'll schedule a staging dry-run tomorrow 09:30 PT and invite the risk / enablement folks to review the sample CSV. Please push any small fixes today so I can include them.

Ava Chen: Thanks — I'll post the dry-run CSV to #customer-success when complete and attach the sampled accounts. If anyone wants a pre-review of the CSV format DM me. :memo:

source.text:
Maya Perez: FYI — LyraHealth submitted their vendor security survey and flagged two items: telemetry collection and BYOK. I uploaded their PDF: https://files.redwood.ai/lyra-survey_v1.pdf
SurveyBot: File uploaded: lyra-survey_v1.pdf
Tom Huang: I'll take BYOK. Can someone paste the exact question wording so I can prepare a concise answer?
Ava Chen: Dropping the excerpts below.
Maya Perez: Q34 Telemetry: "Describe what runtime telemetry is collected and if customer data is included."\nQ58 Keys: "Can customers bring their own keys to encrypt embeddings in Dedicated/Private deployments?"
Tom Huang: For Q58: short answer — Private supports customer-managed KMS/HSM integrations (BYOK). Dedicated uses platform-managed keys by default; enterprise BYOK add-on available. Suggest a 2-line answer + link to docs: https://docs.redwood.ai/security/kms
Leo Patel: Quick note on telemetry — clarify whether they mean service telemetry (latency/ops) vs. content telemetry. We do not log PII in telemetry; only aggregated ops metrics unless customer opts into enhanced telemetry.
Ava Chen: Draft reply for review:\n- Telemetry: We collect operational metrics (latency, error rates, model usage) to maintain SLOs; content or PII are not included in telemetry. Customers can disable telemetry on Private/Dedicated with an ops flag: `telemetry.collection=false`.\n- BYOK: Supported for Private via KMS/HSM integrations; Dedicated customers can request BYOK through our enterprise pack — see docs and sample contract clause.
Rachel Legal: Add a brief sentence referencing our SOC 2 coverage and that BYOK will include audit log access and key-management responsibilities in the contract. I'll prepare the clause.
Maya Perez: Thanks. Ava, can you send this back to Lyra with Rachel cc'd? I'll include the PDF.
Ava Chen: Sending now to LyraHealth portal and copying legal. Will update thread when they reply. :white_check_mark:
SurveyBot: Message posted to LyraHealth (customer-portal): "Redwood CS reply attached. See file lyra-survey_v1_response.pdf"
Maya Perez: Quick follow-up from Lyra (customer portal): "Thanks — two follow-ups: 1) retention period for embeddings; 2) can we get a SOC2 attestation copy?"
Ava Chen: Answering: retention — hosted defaults to 90 days, Dedicated/Private configurable; I'll attach the retention policy doc. SOC2 attestation — will coordinate with Rachel Legal and Ops to provide the latest report under NDA.
Rachel Legal: I'll prepare the SOC2 attestation cover note and share next steps for NDA. 
Maya Perez: Great — keeping this on priority. Closing loop once Lyra confirms.

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick pulse — we need to schedule a Satellite Health bootcamp drop-in for a handful of customers (onboarding + renewal checks). Options: 1) Wed 9:00 AM PST 2) Thu 15:00 UTC 3) Fri 11:00 IST. Who can host and what agenda items should we cover?
Diego Rivera: I can host Thu 15:00 UTC. Please add a short segment on dedicated migration checklist (customers asking about Dedicated sizing).
Samir Patel: Thu works for me too. Also flag: Kestrel Bio shows warning in account health dashboard (usage down, NPS low) — can we carve 15m for renewal concern capture? @Ava Chen @Maya Park
Maya Park: I can run the renewal pulse and a 10m troubleshooting clinic. Proposed agenda: 1) 10m intros + onboarding status 2) 15m dedicated migration checklist 3) 10m renewal pulse & action items 4) 15m Q&A / live demo.
Ava Chen: Love that. Can we keep overall to 50m? customers prefer shorter sessions. Also request: capture feedback form after session for regression monitoring.
Diego Rivera: +1 to 50m. For migration checklist, I'll paste the snippet we use: ```Checklist for Dedicated migration:
- baseline throughput test (1000 reqs)
- quantization profile flagged
- KV cache warmup plan
- rollback strategy + fallback model
```
Samir Patel: I'll invite the account execs for Kestrel and put renewal risk tags in the notes. Also can we add a quick poll in the invite: "Main goal: onboarding / cost optimization / SLA"
Maya Park: I'll create the calendar invite and include the poll & a short pre-read. Can someone drop customer-specific asks in this thread so I can attach them?
Ava Chen: Adding quick asks: Kestrel — want Dedicated perf baseline + security onboarding doc. Nova Health — wants embedding tuning + cost estimate. LumenHealth — RFC about data residency for Private option.
CS Scheduler Bot: Calendar event created: "Satellite enablement — bootcamp drop-in" Thu 15:00 UTC, 50m. Link: https://calendar.redwood-inference.com/e/6a9b — Agenda attached.
Diego Rivera: Thanks! I'll prepare the migration demo (10m).
Samir Patel: I'll coordinate with AMs and leave a 5m follow-up slot for next steps.
Maya Park: Great. I'll circulate invite + pre-read by EOD and post a short feedback form link in the event notes. :thumbsup:
Ava Chen: Perfect — thanks team. If no objections by EOD, we'll lock Thu 15:00 UTC.

source.messages:
Maya Chen: Hey team — pulled together the last two weeks of onboarding notes + NPS followups. Seeing recurring asks and a few blockers. Can we quick-prioritize?
Ethan Park: +1. Can you drop the short list here? I can sync with Product priorities after.
Maya Chen: pasted CSV export snippet into the thread: `customer,session_count,ask_type,priority_votes
Acme Retail,4,per-route-cost-visibility,3
HelixCardio,2,batch-upload-templates,2
NorthBay,6,granular-usage-exports,4`
Lina Gomez: Noted — Acme is on a renewal 30 days out. Per-route cost visibility is standing out as a revenue risk.
Diego Ruiz: Thanks. Quick question: is `per-route-cost-visibility` about UI breakdowns or per-token attribution backend?
Maya Chen: Mostly UI + export, but customer asked for per-route export so finance can map to internal chargebacks. They need CSV with route_id, model, tokens, cost.
Amelia Brooks: From enablement side, we can ship temporary playbook but this keeps coming up. If Prod can scope an MVP (export + basic UI) that should reduce renewal friction.
Ethan Park: MVP scope suggestion: (1) admin CSV export for last 30d, (2) per-route columns for model & tokens, (3) toggle for time window. Anything more is stretch.
Diego Ruiz: Agree with MVP. For backend we already emit token usage by route in daily aggregates, so export endpoint + small UI is ~2 sprint effort.
Lina Gomez: Can we add a severity tag? Acme flagged as P0 for renewal, others are P2. Helps triage.
Maya Chen: tagging applied: Acme (P0), NorthBay (P1), HelixCardio (P2).
feedback-bot: New feedback card created: FB-2026-438 | per-route export | added by Maya Chen | tags: P0, renewal-risk
Ethan Park: Another recurring ask: customers want an easier way to request whitelabel embeddings IDs (renaming routes for orgs). Is that a separate thread?
Maya Chen: Yes — separate but related. For this thread let's keep focus on cost visibility. I'll open a new card for embeddings rename.
Amelia Brooks: On enablement: recommend we draft an email template for Acme explaining timeline + temporary CSV workaround. I can own that once engineering gives ETA.
Diego Ruiz: ETA: if we kick this sprint, target end-of-next-sprint for export + admin UI alpha. Will need a ticket with acceptance criteria: sample CSV, API endpoint, admin role check.
Ethan Park: I'll draft the ticket and include sample CSV. Maya, can you confirm columns and example rows?
Maya Chen: Confirmed columns: `route_id, route_name, model, tokens_total, estimated_cost_usd, time_window_start, time_window_end`. Example row: `r_123,invoice-gen,gpt-4o-mini,14321,0.87,2026-03-01,2026-03-31`.
Lina Gomez: I'll flag to CS playbook: once export alpha is ready, we'll run a guided session with Acme + provide the CSV and walkthrough.
Maya Chen: Thanks. Action items: Ethan to create ticket; Diego to assign dev; Amelia to draft customer email; Lina to schedule guided walkthrough.
Ethan Park: Created ticket #ENG-5621 and linked the FB card: https://redwood.atlassian.com/browse/ENG-5621 — includes sample CSV and acceptance criteria.
feedback-bot: Linked FB-2026-438 -> ENG-5621
Maya Chen: Perfect. One last ask: can Product add a quick prioritization field to the feedback card: impact (1-5) + frequency (1-5)? Makes tradeoffs easier during roadmap reviews.
Diego Ruiz: Done. I set impact=4 frequency=3 on FB-2026-438.
Amelia Brooks: I'll draft the customer-facing note and post here for review before sending.
Lina Gomez: I'll block time on Acme's calendar for the walkthrough after Amelia posts the note.
Maya Chen: Great team. Closing this thread — will follow up with the walkthrough invite and the customer note. :white_check_mark:

source.messages:
Maya Chen: Quick heads-up — Northpoint dedicated pool bring-up is tomorrow 09:00 PST. Need final sign-off on quota numbers and autoscale targets. :calendar:
Ethan Park: ETA on capacity warm GPUs and how many reserved slots we have? I have a smoke script that needs 120 concurrent streams to validate KV cache behavior.
Maya Chen: We reserved 16 GPUs in the pool (8 L40s + 8 A100-like). Proposal: start with quota 10k concurrent tokens/min, burst to 30k for 10m. Can we autoscale from 2->16 instances?
Priya Patel: 2->16 sounds ok but suggest conservative CPU threshold to avoid flapping. Recommend `cpu_threshold: 65%`, `scale_step: 2`, `cooldown: 120s`. Also enable prefix cache warmup for top 20 prompts.
Liam O'Connor: From enablement side: we'll run a partner-facing demo at 13:00 PST. Need a stable 95th p50 latency under 500ms for short chats. If autoscale takes >90s we should have fallback to smaller model.
Ethan Park: Agreed. I'll add a fallback policy to route to the 8-GPU quantized variant if 95th>700ms for 3 consecutive minutes.
Maya Chen: Can someone schedule load test windows in the playbook? I want a stealth soak then a ramp. Plan: 09:15 quick smoke, 10:00 ramp to target QPS, 11:30 chaos test (node kill), 12:00 cooldown.
Priya Patel: I'll own the load scripts. Using k6 + internal harness. Sample ramp command:```k6 run --vus 300 --duration 20m load-tests/northpoint-ramp.js``` We'll do three runs with different prompt mixes.
Ethan Park: Ops checklist (short):
- cluster primed: yes
- autoscaler configured: pending
- fallback model uploaded: yes
- alerting: latency and error-5xx set to pg 1
- runbooks attached to incident channel: /incidents/northpoint
Liam O'Connor: I'll invite the customer enablement lead to the 09:00 call and prepare a 10-min walkthrough for warmup expectations.
Maya Chen: Renewal note: account CSM flagged renewal risk if latency > SLA during demo. We must capture post-test runbook and customer notes. Tagging @Ethan and @Priya for perf logs. :exclamation:
ops-bot: Deploy bot: Dedicated pool allocation request approved for northpoint-pool-01, reservation id res-9842.
Ethan Park: I'll push the autoscale config now to staging. Config snippet:```yaml
autoscale:
  min_replicas: 2
  max_replicas: 16
  cpu_threshold_pct: 65
  scale_step: 2
  stabilization_window_s: 120
  fallback_model: northpoint-quantized-v1
```
Priya Patel: Also enabling KV prefix prefill for top-10 prompts. Will upload prime set to the console under Customer -> Northpoint -> PrimeSets.
Maya Chen: Perfect. Last ask: capture any customer-experience anomalies (token truncation, unexpected rerolls) in the thread and attach sample requests. We need those for the renewal handoff.
Liam O'Connor: Noted. I'll own the notes and assemble a postmortem template if things go sideways.
Maya Chen: Thanks team — see you on the 09:00 sync. :v:

source.text:
Priya Rao: Morning — quick heads-up on the Jade account dedicated pool. Noticed a saturation spike overnight, sustained around ~03:10 UTC. High-level: GPU hours up ~42% WoW, queue depth peaked at 32, spend is trending higher. Can ops/eng confirm root cause and whether this will affect the renewal convo later this week? Attaching the alert logs below.

ops-bot: ```ALERT: pool=dedicated-jade saturation=98% at 2026-03-20T03:10:22Z
queue_depth=32
95p_latency_ms=290
error_rate_pct=0.4
top_consumer=jade-analytics-job-9f2b```

Luca Moretti: On it — found a long-running batch (jade-analytics-job-9f2b) that kicked off at ~02:40 UTC and kept grabbing slots. It wasn't configured as low-priority so it displaced inference traffic. I throttled replicas and set `priorityClass=low` for the remaining runs. Current pool status: saturation ~63%, queue depth 3.
24h GPU hours: 72h (baseline ~50–52h). Estimated incremental spend ~ $820/day. Snippet from the pool metrics:```pool:deds-jade saturation:63%
queue_depth:3
gpu_hours_24h:72
cost_delta_per_day:820```

Ethan Park: Did we see customer-facing latency impact? Jade has a renewal call Friday — we need numbers for the deck.

Luca Moretti: Yes — 95p for `/jade/api/generate` rose from ~210ms -> ~290ms during the peak window. Error rate for that route nudged up ~0.2 percentage points. We pushed a portion of traffic to a smaller model (`redwood-small-v2`) as an emergency fallback which prevented 95p going above ~400ms.

Zoe Chen: Noted. For the renewal I'd recommend: 1) offer a one-time credit (proposal: 20% of this month's dedicated overage) to smooth the conversation; 2) run an optimization workshop with Eng/runtime to tune batch scheduling and autoscale; 3) add preemptible scheduling for heavy analytics jobs so they don't compete with low-latency inference. Also capture feedback: they said the scheduling docs were a bit sparse. :eyes: :calendar:

Priya Rao: Action items — Luca: file a PR to enforce `priorityClass=low` for `jade-analytics` by EOD; Ethan: prepare a 1-pager with latency impact and mitigations for the renewal deck; Zoe: draft the credit proposal and propose two workshop slots (Thu AM or Mon next week). I'll loop in the AM success lead and copy @eng-runtime for the preemptible scheduling ask.

ops-bot: ```STATUS: mitigation applied, saturation down to 63%, queue depth down to 3. Monitor window 1h.```

Priya Rao: Thanks team — keeping this thread as the single source for the renewal notes. If anyone sees a re-spike tag this thread and DM me.

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Quick weekly sync for Acme — noticed a model-mix shift after Friday's rollout. High-level: usage +23% W/W, requests 126k -> 155k, avg spend +9%. Fallbacks spiked and latency hit a 95p of ~780ms at peak. Posting details and next steps below.

Diego Martinez: When exactly was the rollout and what traffic split?

Ava Chen: Fri 11:14 UTC start (canary 20%), reached 100% by 11:40 UTC. Fallback surge started ~12:10 UTC and coincided with a short customer traffic burst.

deploy-bot: Deployment event — redwood-open-v2 rollout -> 100% at 2026-03-19T11:40:12Z :rocket:

Kai Patel: Pulled the telemetry. Key numbers: fallback rate baseline 0.9% -> peak 4.1% (15:12 minute). 95th latency for text-gen increased from 420ms -> 780ms during the spike. Error types: kvcache-miss, attention OOM.

Diego Martinez: Can you paste example error traces / logs so infra can triage quicker?

Ava Chen: copy of a couple lines from logs: `ERROR: kvcache-miss key=usr:acme:session:49f2` and `FATAL: attention_kernel OOM at step=23 model=redwood-open-v2 shards=2`. Also seeing a few `429 fallback` counts.

Olivia Park: (Acme PM) we shipped the bulk-chat feature Friday morning — sessions now include 900–1,800 token histories often. We expected more token consumption but not the degradation. Can you advise if there's a quick mitigation? :grimacing:

Diego Martinez: Short-term options: route long-context sessions to high-memory pool, reduce `max_tokens` for server-side completions, or toggle a smaller quantized variant. Proposed temporary routing rule: 
```
route-rule: if avg_tokens > 512 -> redwood-compat-highmem
else -> redwood-open-v2
```
This should reduce OOMs while we tune caching.

Kai Patel: Cost impact estimate for Friday peak: compute +9% (~$1.7k extra/day @ current unit rates). If we enable prefix/KV caching for ~30% of sessions we estimate fallback down ~1.2pp and cost reduction ~4%. I'll share the numbers on the dashboard.

Ava Chen: Action items I suggest: 1) enable routing rule (Diego) 2) infra to bump highmem pool temporarily (SRE) 3) enable prefix cache for Acme (Kai) 4) customer enablement to nudge prompt trimming where possible (Olivia). I'll coordinate the call and follow-ups.

Diego Martinez: I'll open a Dedicated ticket for pool adjustment and add a canary rollback plan in case the fix regresses latency. :thumbsup:

Olivia Park: Thanks — can we do a 30m call with Acme PMs tomorrow morning PT? They are most available 9am PT.

Ava Chen: Scheduled tentative sync: 2026-03-20 16:00 UTC (9am PT). I'll send the invite and include Diego + Kai.

Kai Patel: Working on a compact dashboard with the spike graphs and token distribution, will drop link here in ~30m.

Ava Chen: Great — please add the estimated cost delta and fallback trend. I'll update this thread after the call with outcomes. Thanks everyone. :pray:

source.messages:
Maya Lin: Weekly account health pulse — token usage + throughput snapshot for LumenHealth, Cobalt Labs, NovaRetail. Please drop WoW & QoQ deltas, p50/p95 latency, error %, and spend delta. Flag renewal risk and next action. TL;DR by EOD please :wave:

Jonas Patel: LumenHealth — tokens WoW +12%, QoQ +38% (onboarding ramp). Throughput up, average p50 85ms p95 290ms. Error rate 0.7% (bulk import timeouts at ~03:10 UTC causing spikes). Spend +22% QoQ. Renewal risk: low. Action: scheduled enablement w/eng for caching + prefix reuse Fri 11am PT. Paging @Ava Chen for batching knobs.

Priya Singh: Cobalt Labs — tokens WoW -4%, QoQ +6%. Throughput steady but p95 stretched: p50 120ms p95 450ms. Error rate 2.6%, mostly 429 rate-limit during short bursts; they fell back to smaller variant which reduced QoE. Spend -5% WoW (they throttled). Renewal risk: medium — concerned about cost predictability. Action: propose quota smoothing + adaptive routing; scheduling cost review with customer on Thu. Dashboard: https://console.redwood/in/orgs/cobalt/usage

Ava Chen: I pushed a temporary route rule to fallback earlier to rwd-small-v2 to avoid 429 cascades. Also added a conservative batching policy for Cobalt (max_batch_ms=30) to reduce tail latencies. Can someone drop a short traffic histogram so I can iterate parameters? :white_check_mark:

Jonas Patel: sample traffic histogram (last 24h):
```
peak qps windows: [120, 340, 290, 45]
median request size: 650 tokens
95th request size: 2800 tokens
spike pattern: 02:50-03:20 UTC daily (import jobs)
```
Route policy suggestion applied: 
```
route_policy:
  max_batch_ms: 30
  prefer_cache: true
  fallback_model: "rwd-small-v2"
  burst_shaping: {window_ms: 500, max_concurrent: 60}
```

Maya Lin: NovaRetail — tokens WoW +55% (promo week), QoQ +120%. Throughput large increase but p95 still healthy: p50 70ms p95 180ms. Error rate 0.2%. Spend +90% QoQ (promo-driven). Renewal risk: high-but-positive (they'll likely request Dedicated; moving from Hosted to committed next quarter). Action: scope Dedicated sizing meeting Tues 10am PT; @Priya add to renewal tracker and prep committed SKU options.

Priya Singh: Added NovaRetail to renewal tracker (sheet updated). I'll prepare three SKU options and a migration checklist.

title: #customer-success: 1968123456
preview:
Maya Lin: Weekly account health pulse — token usage + throughput snapshot for LumenHealth, Cobalt Labs, NovaRetail. Please drop WoW & QoQ deltas, p50/p95 latency, error %, and spend delta. Flag renewal risk and next action. TL;DR by EOD please :wave:

Jonas Patel: LumenHealth — tokens WoW +12%, QoQ +38% (onboarding ramp). Throughput up, average p50 85ms p95 290ms. Error rate 0.7% (bulk import timeouts at ~03:10 UTC causing spikes). Spend +22% QoQ. Renewal risk: low. Action: scheduled enablement w/eng for caching + prefix reuse Fri 11am PT. Paging @Ava Chen for batching knobs.

Priya Singh: Cobalt Labs — tokens WoW -4%, QoQ +6%. Throughput steady but p95 stretched: p50 120ms p95 450ms. Error rate 2.6%, mostly 429 rate-limit during short bursts; they fell back to smaller variant which reduced QoE. Spend -5% WoW (they throttled). Renewal risk: medium — concerned about cost predictability. Action: propose quota smoothing + adaptive routing; scheduling cost review with customer on Thu. Dashboard: https://console.redwood/in/orgs/cobalt/usage

Ava Chen: I pushed a temporary route rule to fallback earlier to rwd-small-v2 to avoid 429 cascades. Also added a conservative batching policy for Cobalt (max_batch_ms=30) to reduce tail latencies. Can someone drop a short traffic histogram so I can iterate parameters? :white_check_mark:

Jonas Patel: sample traffic histogram (last 24h):
```
peak qps windows: [120, 340, 290, 45]
median request size: 650 tokens
95th request size: 2800 tokens
spike pattern: 02:50-03:20 UTC daily (import jobs)
```
Route policy suggestion applied: 
```
route_policy:
  max_batch_ms: 30
  prefer_cache: true
  fallback_model: "rwd-small-v2"
  burst_shaping: {window_ms: 500, max_concurrent: 60}
```

Maya Lin: NovaRetail — tokens WoW +55% (promo week), QoQ +120%. Throughput large increase but p95 still healthy: p50 70ms p95 180ms. Error rate 0.2%. Spend +90% QoQ (promo-driven). Renewal risk: high-but-positive (they'll likely request Dedicated; moving from Hosted to committed next quarter). source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick plan - running a 6-week "Cohort Accelerator" preview for 8 mid-market finance customers (renewal-risk + high-impact). Window: Apr 11 start, onboarding Apr 11-15. Need enrollments, enablement leads, and a tight feedback loop. Volunteers? :wave:

Liam Patel: I can handle metrics + survey. Will want TtFS, feature ask tags, and a quick NPS-ish question. Share customer roster & prompt examples.

Sophie Rivera: I'll lead the onboarding calls. Recommend 45m guided + 15m open troubleshooting. I'll make a standard deck + runbook. Dry run Wed ok for everyone?

Diego Morales: I'll allocate Dedicated pools. Current availability Tue/Thu 9-11am PT; will reserve Sat low-traffic window for scale test. Need expected qps and exact dates.

Ava Chen: Draft email & onboarding agenda below. Please edit.

Ava Chen: ```Subject: Invite: Redwood Cohort Accelerator (finance) - preview access

Hi {{first_name}},

We're inviting a small group to preview Redwood's Cohort Accelerator. You'll get: reserved capacity for 6 weeks, a 45-min guided onboarding call, and two short feedback checkpoints (wk2 & wk5).

Pick a slot: https://cal.example/rdw-preview

Data handling: KMS-backed keys, logs retained per policy. Reply if you'd like an NDA.

Best,
Ava on behalf of Redwood CS
```

Sophie Rivera: Suggest adding a one-line "what success looks like" for customers and an item about data retention. Also shorten subject line to help opens.

Liam Patel: Survey plan: 6 closed q's + 1 open. Drafting now; will include a prompt-failure example box so we collect real examples. Also propose weekly usage checkpoint (5 min) to check ramp.

Diego Morales: For autoscale planning assume 1-3 rps per customer peak. I'll set up fallback to hosted replicas if Dedicated approaches 80% util. Will post runbook snippet.

cs_bot: Reminder: Cohort Accelerator playbook https://rdw.internal/playbooks/cohort-accelerator (enrollment template, deck, survey). Also auto-reminder: set followups at wk2 and wk5.

Ava Chen: Volunteers: Liam (metrics + survey), Sophie (enablement deck + calls), Diego (capacity + runbook). I'll send the invite batch tomorrow & add the roster to the shared sheet. Dry run Wed 10am PT - everyone ok? :thumbsup:

Liam Patel: 10am Wed works. I'll bring the survey draft.

Sophie Rivera: yup. I'll bring the deck and a troubleshooting checklist (auth, prompt poisoning, quantization edge cases).

Diego Morales: joining. Will add quick ops steps: rollback to hosted model, notify CS, throttle by customer if needed.

Ava Chen: Great. Action items: 1) Liam - share survey + TtFS metric spec by EOD, 2) Sophie - draft deck + FAQ by Tue, 3) Diego - reserve pools + ops runbook by Tue. I'll finalize email and roster. Thanks team! :tada:

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick capture from HelixCardio onboarding retro earlier today — writing up raw asks so we don't lose anything. TL;DR customer is happy with latency but running into visibility + control gaps.

Ava Chen: Raw asks (verbatim / summarized):
- "per-route cost breakdown per model" — they want to see which route is burning budget.
- "granular RBAC for routes and models" — legal wants only certain teams to call prod models.
- "retry/debug logs on streaming failures" — hard to triage intermittent disconnects.
- "embedding drift detection" — embeddings for search are degrading after model swap.
- "model fallback tunables" — want to control when we fallback to cheaper variants.
- "onboarding docs: quickstart for dedicated customers" — missing checklist for infra/network steps.

Miguel Torres: Nice summary. Can we prioritize 1-2 for Q2? Renewal convo next week, want a list to promise if we surface as committed work. :eyes:

Jordan Patel: Suggest we do an impact x effort quick matrix. My initial read: cost breakdown = high impact / med effort; RBAC = high impact / high effort; retry logs = med impact / low effort; embedding drift = high impact / med/high effort; fallback tunables = high impact / low-med effort; docs = low effort / med impact.

Priya Singh: Adding direct customer quotes (for context):
- "We can't justify volume until we can show where the spend goes."
- "Our security team will not accept broad keys for model calls."

Liam O'Riley: From eng POV: retry/debug logs and fallback tunables look like the fastest wins. Retry logs -> add structured events + request_id propagation, ~2 sprints. Fallback tunables -> config + route-level policy, ~1 sprint. Per-route cost needs backend work + ui, ~3-4 sprints.

Jordan Patel: OK. Proposal: mark 'per-route cost' and 'RBAC' as required for renewal (if we can't commit, we escalate). But target delivery: fallback tunables + retry logs as Q2 quickships. Can someone own triage + ticketing?

Ava Chen: I'll own the triage for CS side — will convert customer notes into acceptance criteria and hand to Jordan for PM prioritization.

Jordan Patel: I'll create tickets and add labels. Draft tickets being created: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/RED-4512 (fallback-tunables), https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/RED-4513 (retry-logs). Will add priority and link to this thread.

cs-bot: Action logged: "create:triage_note" by Ava Chen on 1963001123

Miguel Torres: Please add an "renewal_risk" flag on the per-route-cost item — we need that surfaced in the renewal tracker.

Ava Chen: Done. Updated triage note with renewal_risk=high and customer severity examples.

Jordan Patel: Quick checklist I'll add to each ticket (copy/paste):
```
- customer_impact: <quote>
- acceptance_criteria: <what we will demo>
- owner: <name>
- estimate: <sprint points>
- renewal_risk_flag: <high/med/low>
```

Priya Singh: Also adding: copy of onboarding notes and the customer's recommended rollout plan (they prefer canary on dedicated first). I'll drop the doc in the CS folder.

Liam O'Riley: If we go with canary for fallback tunables, we should add metrics to alert on quality regressions. Adding to ticket.

Ava Chen: Thanks team. Next steps: Jordan to add tickets + labels; Liam to add engineering estimates; Ava to push triage note and schedule a 30m sync with HelixCardio if Jordan flags we can commit.

Miguel Torres: :thumbsup: — please ping me once tickets are live; I'm on the renewal call Thurs.

Jordan Patel: Will do.

Ava Chen: Ending thread, will update when docs/tickets exist.

source.messages:
Maya Chen: Kicking off Orbiton onboarding — short sync to confirm owners, success criteria, and 30-day plan. Please respond with owners.

Maya Chen: Success criteria suggestion:
- Staging integration + passing smoke tests
- Demo latency p95 < 300ms under demo load
- Two enablement sessions recorded
- Security checklist acknowledged by customer SOC

Ethan Park: I can own the integration work and staging endpoints. ETA Day 6 for first test endpoint.

Ravi Kumar: I'll handle security checklist + pen-test coordination. Need their SOC contact and any requested dataflow diagrams.

Sofia Alvarez: CS ops will create the 30/60/90 playbook and track KPIs: activation, errors, and support tickets. Ill add a finance checkpoint Day 10 for PO path.

Ivy Tran: Enablement owner here. Planning two sessions: dev walkthrough and support runbook; will record and drop short clips for execs. Target weeks: May 12 & May 19.

OnboardBot: Calendar created: "Orbiton Onboarding - Weekly Stand" Tue May 5 10:00 PDT. Invitees: Maya, Ethan, Ravi, Sofia, Ivy.

Ethan Park: sample curl for staging (temp token):
```
curl -X POST https://staging-api.redwood.local/v1/generate 
  -H \"Authorization: Bearer stg_orbit_9ab\" 
  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" 
  -d '{\"model\":\"redwood-lite\",\"input\":\"test\"}'
```

Ravi Kumar: Risk log: SOC review could delay pilot if pen-test scope not approved by Day 12. Also note data residency question flagged in contract.

Maya Chen: Action items captured:
- Ethan: staging endpoint + sample curl (Due Day 6)
- Ravi: security checklist + scope doc (Due Day 5)
- Ivy: enablement schedule + recordings (Dates May 12 & 19)
- Sofia: playbook + finance checkpoint (Day 10)

Maya Chen: Please react with :white_check_mark: when you confirm your items.
