source.messages:
Nadia Ahmed: Heads up: HarborFlow (mid-market, renewal in 5d) flagged at-risk. Low adoption of scheduled jobs, reported spikes in token cost after a recent feature toggle. They mentioned timeouts on some batch exports. Need a concise save play we can run before renewal.
Ethan Park: Any telemetry links? Is it a new integration or change in usage patterns?
Riley Morgan: Quick look: new webhook consumer enabled last week; requests are long (avg 12k tokens) and some retries loop. Also their feature toggle accidentally set verbose logging -> extra tokens.
ops-bot: Alert: HarborFlow export retries detected, duplicate rate 6k/hr. See incident HF-2211.
Samir Desai: Two parallel tracks: immediate mitigation (rate-limit + toggle revert), and a short commercial/enablement offer for reassurance. Recommend 10-day trial credits + engineer support.
Jules Kim: I'll draft a short customer message and an enablement checklist. We should offer a scoped temporary SLA on export route if they accept remediation.
Ethan Park: Technical fixes I can push now: 1) revert verbose logging setting, 2) apply a temporary per-route throttle (300 r/s), 3) enable token cap for export endpoint (max_tokens=4000). All reversible.
Riley Morgan: I'll prepare a preload for common prefixes and a quick script to dedupe webhook retries. Also will surface p95 latency before/after.
Nadia Ahmed: Commercial: propose 2-week 20% credit + two 1-hour enablement sessions. If they commit to a 3-month reserved plan within 30d, convert credits to prepaid. Also include opt to keep one critical route on reserved capacity at current price.
Samir Desai: If they push to downgrade, use a graded downgrade: reduce non-customer-facing exports to best-effort while keeping storefront and billing routes on standard SLA. Capture that in a downgrade matrix.
Jules Kim: Draft message:
``
Hi HarborFlow team,
We saw increased export sizes and some retry loops after your recent toggle. Immediate steps we propose: (1) 30-min triage call, (2) revert verbose logging and apply temporary throttle, (3) 2-week cost credit while we validate. We can also enable a temporary SLA for the export route during the trial.
Thanks, Nadia + Redwood CS
```
Ethan Park: Reverted toggle and applied throttle. Monitoring shows duplicate rate dropped ~92%. Will keep sampling.
Riley Morgan: Dedupe script staged; ready for prod push after call. Preload script ready too.
Nadia Ahmed: Owners & deadlines:
- Jules: customer email + one-pager (owner) - due in 4h
- Ethan: toggles & throttles (owner) - done
- Riley: dedupe + preload + metrics (owner) - push after call
- Samir: commercial terms & downgrade matrix (owner) - draft in 6h
Call scheduled for 9am PT tomorrow. If no agreement within 48h post-call, escalate to @Head_CS.
Samir Desai: Draft uploaded to shared drive: https://drive.redwood/hf-save-1952. Added downgrade matrix and conversion incentives.
Jules Kim: One-pager and email sent to Nadia for review. Also included a short NPS + friction survey to run after trial.
Nadia Ahmed: Great work. If call successful, target conversion to a 3-month reserved uplift. If they instead choose downgrade, follow the staged downgrade to preserve revenue-critical routes. Tag updates here.

source.messages:
Nora Williams: Quick thread  Orion QBR on Fri. Need to finalize deck, risk grade, and the exact exec ask. Can everyone post updates by EOD Wed?

Ethan Park: Working on usage + SLA slide. Will drop a v1 to the drive tomorrow AM. Need someone to sanity-check the churn overlay. :mag_right:

Priya Shah: I can take the churn overlay and onboarding KPIs. Pulling Gainsight + telemetry now. ETA Wed 11am.

Diego Alvarez: Heads up  enablement wants a short 8-min demo of the self-serve runbook. Recommend ops appendix, not the exec summary.

Nora Williams: Ops appendix makes sense. Diego  please prepare 1 summary slide + 1 screenshot/GIF (compressed). Keep it <40s.

Ethan Park: Uploaded draft deck: https://drive.redwood/orion/qbr/draft (comments on). Please add short inline notes only.

Priya Shah: Onboarding snapshot for slides: Seats: 980; Active MAUs (30d): 712; Onboard complete (core): 81%; Avg TTF success: 5.9d.

Nora Williams: Renewal posture  CSAT steady but two heavy-usage teams show weekly dip. Setting Orion to Amber (watch). Proposed mitigations: targeted enablement, exec sync, 4-week usage credits pilot.

Ethan Park: Draft exec ask (short): "Request 30-min strategic sync to review usage trends, approve 4-week credits pilot for at-risk cohorts, and endorse quarterly enablement workshops." Keep as 3 bullets.

Priya Shah: Edit: say "4-week usage credits pilot" and add metric: "+12% active users in targeted cohort".

Diego Alvarez: Enablement will ask for 2x 45-min hands-on sessions. Adding to slide appendix.

QBRBot: Reminder: QBR template auto-check in 48 hrs. Missing exec ask or >12 slides will fail.

Nora Williams: Action items recap:
- Ethan: finalize deck (due Thu 9am)
- Priya: final metrics + churn graph (due Wed 11am)
- Diego: enablement slide + GIF (due Wed 4pm)
- Samira (CSM): confirm exec availability & send invite (Thu EOD)
- Nora: finalize exec ask and coordinate demo (Thu 3pm)

Ethan Park: One more ask  slide 7 should synthesize customer feedback into 3 themes (2 bullets each max). Who wants to do a quick themes edit?

Priya Shah: I'll synthesize quotes into 3 themes by Wed noon.

Nora Williams: Great. If risk moves to Red, well schedule a pre-QBR risk huddle 24-36 hrs before. Keep monitoring the two teams flagged. Ping me for blockers. :thumbsup:

source.messages:
Maya Chen: FYI — I'm starting a proactive perf check for Larkspur (onboarding account). Seeing p95 ~740ms on gen calls and unit cost ~ $12 / 1M tokens. Renewal conversation in ~9 weeks, flagging as moderate risk if we don't show improvements.
Ethan Park: Can you paste the current model + runtime config?
Maya Chen: model=redwood/long-llm-7b @v2, quantization=none, region=us-west-2, batching=1, kv_cache=disabled, routing=default-tiered
Ravi Iyer: Quick read: we've seen this profile before. My recommendation: 1) swap to rq4g-v2 (4-bit quant) as a canary, 2) enable per-session KV prefix caching, 3) route lower-priority analytic calls to spot/dedicated-cheap pool in us-west-2, 4) do 10% canary -> 50% rollout with automatic rollback on quality delta >1.5%
Maya Chen: Ran a small sim on their prompt set — rq4g-v2 numbers look promising: p95 drops ~740ms -> ~420ms, median latency shaved ~35%, estimated token cost -28%. Note: we saw occasional tokenization variance on >1k tokens.
Jo Martinez: Customer ask from last call: they need transcripts to be near-deterministic for billing downstream. Any expected regressions there with 4-bit quant? We should capture that in the eval.
Ethan Park: I'll create a focused eval harness: golden prompt set (50 examples) + metrics: exact-match for critical fields, embedding drift, and semantic similarity. Will run baseline (v2) vs rq4g-v2 and post results here. ETA: 24h.
Ravi Iyer: Rollout snippet for ops (example):
```
routing:
  - rule: low_priority_to_spot
    match: request.tags.priority == 'low'
    target_pool: us-west-2-spot
model_override:
  base: redwood/long-llm-7b@v2
  canary: rq4g-v2
canary: 0.10
quality_monitor:
  delta_threshold: 0.015
  check_set: larkspur-golden-50
```
Maya Chen: I'll coordinate the enablement session with Larkspur — proposing Wed 11am PT. Jo can you own scheduling and invite SE? @Jo Martinez
Jo Martinez: Got it, I'll send invite and include a short agenda: what changed, expected perf delta, QA checklist, rollback plan.
Ethan Park: Uploaded initial eval run: baseline -> rq4g-v2 diff: EM drop 0.8%, embedding cosine avg 0.992, hallucination flags +0.2%. Attaching full CSV to workspace link: https://redwood.workspaces/s/teams/larkspur/evals/1940129876
Maya Chen: Nice. Given those deltas, ROI looks good. I'll draft the customer-facing note: perf changes, small QA caveat, suggested canary window of 2 days.
deploy-bot: Canary deployment started for project:larkspur canary_id:can-1940129876 at 1940131200
Ravi Iyer: Monitoring hooks: added trace for kv-hit-rate and model-variance. Alert if kv-hit < 30% or p95 increases above baseline.
Jo Martinez: After enablement we'll capture feedback items in the renewal playbook and route any feature asks to product.
Maya Chen: Action items: 1) Ethan — finalize eval & share detailed report (due +24h); 2) Jo — schedule enablement (due tomorrow); 3) Ravi — apply routing + monitoring canary (done); 4) Maya — send customer note post-canary. Assign and ack here.
Ethan Park: Ack.
Jo Martinez: Ack.
Ravi Iyer: Ack.
Maya Chen: :thumbsup:
deploy-bot: Canary health check passed at 1940132300 (p95=410ms, kv-hit=47%, quality-delta=0.9%)
Maya Chen: Thanks team — will update account health dashboard and flag improvement to Renewal lead.
Jo Martinez: I'll capture the customer's feedback during enablement and add to renewal risk tracker.
Maya Chen: Closing thread for now, will re-open if canary needs rollback or customer escalates.

Ethan Park: I'll create a focused eval harness: golden prompt set (50 examples) + metrics: exact-match for critical fields, embedding drift, and semantic similarity. Will run baseline (v2) vs rq4g-v2 and post results here. ETA: 24h.
Ravi Iyer: Rollout snippet for ops (example):
```
routing:
  - rule: low_priority_to_spot
    match: request.tags.priority == 'low'
    target_pool: us-west-2-spot
model_override:
  base: redwood/long-llm-7b@v2
  canary: rq4g-v2
canary: 0.10
quality_monitor:
  delta_threshold: 0.015
  check_set: larkspur-golden-50
```
Maya Chen: I'll coordinate the enablement session with Larkspur — proposing Wed 11am PT. Jo can you own scheduling and invite SE? @Jo Martinez
Jo Martinez: Got it, I'll send invite and include a short agenda: what changed, expected perf delta, QA checklist, rollback plan.
Ethan Park: Uploaded initial eval run: baseline -> rq4g-v2 diff: EM drop 0.8%, embedding cosine avg 0.992, hallucination flags +0.2%. Attaching full CSV to workspace link: https://redwood.workspaces/s/teams/larkspur/evals/1940129876
Maya Chen: Nice. Given those deltas, ROI looks good. I'll draft the customer-facing note: perf changes, small QA caveat, suggested canary window of 2 days.
deploy-bot: Canary deployment started for project:larkspur canary_id:can-1940129876 at 1940131200
Ravi Iyer: Monitoring hooks: added trace for kv-hit-rate and model-variance. Alert if kv-hit < 30% or p95 increases above baseline.
Jo Martinez: After enablement we'll capture feedback items in the renewal playbook and route any feature asks to product.
Maya Chen: Action items: 1) Ethan — finalize eval & share detailed report (due +24h); 2) Jo — schedule enablement (due tomorrow); 3) Ravi — apply routing + monitoring canary (done); 4) Maya — send customer note post-canary. Assign and ack here.
Ethan Park: Ack.
Jo Martinez: Ack.
Ravi Iyer: Ack.
Maya Chen: :thumbsup:
deploy-bot: Canary health check passed at 1940132300 (p95=410ms, kv-hit=47%, quality-delta=0.9%)
Maya Chen: Thanks team — will update account health dashboard and flag improvement to Renewal lead.
Jo Martinez: I'll capture the customer's feedback during enablement and add to renewal risk tracker.
Maya Chen: Closing thread for now, will re-open if canary needs rollback or customer escalates.

source.messages:
Aisha Khan: FYI customer ACME just escalated — after the v3.2-quant rollout their invoice-parsing accuracy dropped ~30% for a specific prompt set. Internal evals (same test suite) show no regression. Can we triage? :eyes:
Ben Carter: assigning to eng-ml + runtime. Do we have the customer prompt and an example failure?
Aisha Khan: pasted below (sanitized). They say it used to return a structured JSON with fields, now sometimes returns free text or misses the 'invoice_total' field entirely. Customer prompt:
```
Please extract invoice fields: vendor, invoice_number, invoice_date, invoice_total
Invoice text: "ACME Corp\u00A01001
Date: 2025-11-05
Total: $4,320.00"
```
Note: the space between 'ACME Corp' and '1001' appears to be a non-breaking space (\u00A0) in the original customer payload.
Priya Nair: interesting — internal tests probably used normal spaces. Tokenization/normalization differences could explain mismatch. Did rollout change tokenizer or quantization that affects token boundaries?
Evan Li: runtime didn't change tokenizer intentionally. v3.2-quant included new fused attention kernel and a different packing path. Quantization can change decoding slightly. But 30% is big. Do we have the exact API input/response from the customer?
Aisha Khan: customer sent their raw request (sanitized):
```json
{"model":"redwood-v3-quant","input":"Please extract invoice fields: vendor, invoice_number, invoice_date, invoice_total\nInvoice text: \"ACME Corp\u00A01001\nDate: 2025-11-05\nTotal: $4,320.00\"","temperature":0.0}
```
Customer observed response:
```json
{"vendor":"ACME Corp1001","invoice_number":"","invoice_date":"2025-11-05","invoice_total":"4320.00"}
```
Expected (pre-rollout):
```json
{"vendor":"ACME Corp","invoice_number":"1001","invoice_date":"2025-11-05","invoice_total":"4320.00"}
```
support-bot: Created ticket #CS-4321 and linked ACME's logs.
Ben Carter: can we reproduce locally by sending the exact payload (with NBSP) to the canary and to previous model variant? Priya, can you try tokenizing that string in our tokenizer to see token boundaries?
Priya Nair: running tokenizer now. Quick result: when the NBSP is present, tokenizer produces a single token spanning "Corp\u00A01001" for the v3 tokenizer config. With normal space it splits into two tokens. That would explain vendor+invoice_number concatenation at generation with deterministic decoding.
Evan Li: good find. So internal evals likely normalized whitespace before tests. ACME's prompt preserved NBSP. Two paths: (1) update our normalization on ingest to normalize NBSPs, (2) add a check in rollout to run customer prompt sets unchanged. Which is safer short-term?
Aisha Khan: customer asks for quick mitigation — they prefer a patch on our side so they don't need to change their pipeline. Can we enable a preprocessing normalization for text inputs for hosted API only?
Ben Carter: engineering wise, adding a sanitizer that normalizes NBSP -> regular space before tokenization is low-risk. But need to audit for potential side effects (languages where NBSP matters). We can gate it behind a feature flag for the hosted API.
Priya Nair: I'll prototype a normalized path and run the customer's prompt set across canary vs prod. Also will re-run internal evals with NBSP variants to estimate regression sensitivity.
Evan Li: +1. Also check the quantized model's tokenization behavior doesn't differ across builds — upstream tokenizer weights/config mismatch is possible. Log the tokenizer config used in the model artifact.
Aisha Khan: communicating to customer: "we're investigating; temporary mitigation available (flag + canary) — we'll follow up in <4h with plan." They confirmed that's acceptable.
Ben Carter: action items: Priya prototype sanitizer + tests, Evan validate model tokenizer config and artifact consistency, QA run customer's prompt set with NBSP and normal space on v3.1 and v3.2-quant, Support update CS-4321 with ETA.
Priya Nair: started local repro: curl to canary shows the same concatenation. Example curl:
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"redwood-v3-quant","input":"Please extract invoice fields: vendor, invoice_number, invoice_date, invoice_total\nInvoice text: \"ACME Corp\u00A01001\nDate: 2025-11-05\nTotal: $4,320.00\"","temperature":0.0}'
```
Response reproduces customer case. Running tokenizer patch now.
Evan Li: if sanitizer fixes it, we should rollout to hosted quickly and then schedule a model-artifact audit for Dedicated/Private customers with a note about tokenizer parity.
Aisha Khan: customer wants notes for their compliance team. Can we include a short explanation of the root cause and patch plan in CS-4321?
Ben Carter: yes, include: root cause (NBSP tokenized as part of token -> concatenation), short term fix (input normalization on hosted API), medium term (artifact/tokenizer parity checks + eval harness include raw customer prompt sets), long term (add whitespace-varied tests in release gating).
Priya Nair: patch works in local canary — vendor and invoice_number separate now. I'll open PR and request urgent review.
support-bot: PR #8765 opened by priya.nair (label: urgent, changelog: input-normalization).
Evan Li: great. Once PR merges, let's run the customer's full prompt set, then canary deploy and notify ACME after verification.
Aisha Khan: thanks everyone. I'll keep CS-4321 updated and share reproduction steps with ACME for transparency. :handshake:

source.content:
Nora Blake: Quick sync  Doing an EBR for Northbridge next Tue 11am PT. Need: final slides, exec one-pager, and an updated risk remediation table by Mon EOD. Who can own what?

Ethan Park: Ill own the retention & health slide. Will pull last 30/60/90d cohorts and add a short narrative.

Sana Iqbal: I'll draft the exec one-pager (3 bullets + asks) and collate cost estimates with Finance. ETA Mon noon.

Diego Ramos: I'll handle the demo + fallback assets. Live demo preferred; fallback will be a 90s video + screenshots. Uploading to PV by Mon 4pm.

qbr-bot: Calendar event "Northbridge EBR - Dry Run" created for Tue 11:00 PT. Reminder 30m prior.

Nora Blake: Runbook snippet: 
```
1) Nora - Context + top metric (3m)
2) Ethan - Health & churn drivers (7m)
3) Sana - Renewal risk score + asks (5m)
4) Diego - Demo + fallback (8m)
5) Q&A + actions (12m)

Actions:
- Ethan: finalize charts + upload CSV (Mon 10am)
- Sana: one-pager draft + cost numbers (Mon noon)
- Diego: fallback video + screenshots (Mon 4pm)
- Nora: consolidate action list and share invites (Mon EOD)
```

Ethan Park: Small ask - show both raw ticket counts and normalized per DAU. Will annotate slide.

Sana Iqbal: Agree. Also switching "high-risk" label to numeric 1-5 score for clarity.

Diego Ramos: Running a staging demo smoke test Fri. If flakey, we'll default to the recorded video.

Nora Blake: Cool. If no blockers, aim to do an internal dry run Tue 9am PT then the exec slice at 11. Ping here for any last-minute issues. :rocket:

source.messages:
Maria Gomez: Quick weekly check-in for Quasar Health — they had 3 repeat 503s Friday afternoon (approx 15:02 UTC) during a billing spike. I logged the customer ticket (CS-8742). Can we confirm owners and next steps?

Ben Carter: I saw that. Root cause looks like connection pool exhaustion on the dedicated gateway. Error sample: `ERR_HTTP2_STREAM_CANCEL` in the logs.

Tim Liu: Assigned infra action. I'll take ownership of the pool tuning and short-term mitigations. Planning: bump pool size + add stricter circuit-breaker.

Aisha Patel: Thanks @Tim. I'll own the customer comms and update CS-8742 with the mitigation ETA. Also flagging renewal risk if this repeats.

Ben Carter: Short-term patch pushed to staging now. Deploy Bot: canary results?

Deploy Bot: Canary deploy to quasar-canary-2 completed at 2029-11-02T16:18:30Z — 0 errors in 20m smoke tests. :white_check_mark:

Tim Liu: Rolling the config to prod tonight at 22:00 UTC with a 10-minute canary. Action items: (1) increase pool from 200 -> 400, (2) add connection backoff on 5xx, (3) enable prefix KV cache for billing route.

Ben Carter: Adding a retry-after header handler on the client side to reduce retry storms. I'll open infra ticket INF-2198 and link to CS-8742.

Aisha Patel: I'll schedule a 15-min sync with the Quasar PM on Monday to walk through mitigations and offer an enablement doc for their engineers (best practices for retries + idempotency).

Maria Gomez: Ownership summary for the thread: Tim = infra/migration, Ben = client-side mitigations + INF-2198, Aisha = customer comms + enablement, Maria = escalation owner and renewal flag. Please confirm and drop any blockers.

Tim Liu: Confirmed. No blockers from infra, just need escalation window tonight (22:00-22:30 UTC).

Ben Carter: Confirmed. Will have INF-2198 link by EOD.

Aisha Patel: Confirmed. I'll send the calendar invite for Mon 10:00am PT and update CS-8742 with the interim workaround.

Maria Gomez: Excellent. Follow-ups: Aisha to post customer-facing notes, Tim to update post-deploy metrics in #eng-monitoring, Ben to attach client patch. We'll reconvene if repeat errors show up. :rocket:

source.messages:
Maya Patel: Heads up team — GreenMeadow just hit our alert thresholds. Usage down ~48% over the last 30 days, seat activity below SLA, and they swapped procurement lead last week. They’re running a side eval of AtlasAI (heard from AE). Need quick triage and owner assignments.
Diego Ramos: Thanks Maya. Do we have a breakdown by workspace/feature? Is the drop sudden or gradual? cc @Ava for logs.
Ava Chen: Pulling queries now, will drop raw numbers. Quick snippet: `SELECT date, active_users FROM usage WHERE account = 'greenmeadow' ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 60;` seeing a sharp drop starting ~2026-02-10. Workspace 'ops' impacted most.
Jordan Kim: Noted. My read —1) usage drop + procurement change = higher churn risk, 2) competitive eval means pricing/feature gaps in perception. Recommend immediate actions: schedule exec check-in (Diego), technical deep-dive to unblock (Ava), and a targeted enablement + ROI deck for new procurement (Jordan/Maya).
Maya Patel: Agreed. Diego — can you own the exec outreach? Suggest reaching out with a short wins summary and propose a 30-min sync. Jordan — can you prepare a 1-pager comparing our throughput/cost vs AtlasAI (talking points, not public doc)? Ava — can you validate if any recent infra changes correlate with the ops workspace drop?
Diego Ramos: I’ll reach out to the new procurement lead and their CTO. Drafting email now, will ping for review. ETA EOD.
Ava Chen: No infra deploys in that window. Saw a config tweak in their integration (retry/backoff) that reduced call volume — I’ll capture exact change and a repro.
Jordan Kim: Starting the 1-pager. Will include token-cost comparison, latency numbers from our catalog, and a small appendix with suggested cost-saving configs (batching + caching).
Maya Patel: Also worth offering a short remediation workshop (30-45m) to walk their platform team through caching and batching tips — offer 1 complimentary session to re-engage. If they mention price, offer a limited credit for a performance pilot.
Diego Ramos: Sounds good. I’ll put the workshop in the outreach. If procurement pushes back, escalate to AE and CS lead.
engagement-bot: Reminder set by Maya Patel for follow-up on 1847654321 in 3 days.

title: #customer-success: 1847654321
preview:
Maya Patel: Heads up team — GreenMeadow just hit our alert thresholds. Usage down ~48% over the last 30 days, seat activity below SLA, and they swapped procurement lead last week. They’re running a side eval of AtlasAI (heard from AE). Need quick triage and owner assignments.
Diego Ramos: Thanks Maya. Do we have a breakdown by workspace/feature? Is the drop sudden or gradual? cc @Ava for logs.
Ava Chen: Pulling queries now, will drop raw numbers. Quick snippet: `SELECT date, active_users FROM usage WHERE account = 'greenmeadow' ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 60;` seeing a sharp drop starting ~2026-02-10. Workspace 'ops' impacted most.
Jordan Kim: Noted. My read —1) usage drop + procurement change = higher churn risk, 2) competitive eval means pricing/feature gaps in perception. Recommend immediate actions: schedule exec check-in (Diego), technical deep-dive to unblock (Ava), and a targeted enablement + ROI deck for new procurement (Jordan/Maya).

source.text:
Ava Chen: Hey team — NimbleHealth asked for a recurring enablement slot for next quarter. They want a mix of weekly office hours + monthly deep-dive workshops. Can we pick a standing weekly 60m window and one monthly 90m workshop?

Marcus Lee: I can do Tuesdays 10am PT most weeks. Works better than afternoons for my calendar.

Riley Gomez: Tuesdays 10am PT +1 from me. For the monthly workshop I'd like to cover routing/fallbacks one month and prompt engineering the next.

Priya Patel: Can we confirm timezone for the customer? NimbleHealth is spread across US/EU — they'd prefer a slot that hits late-morning PT / evening CET. Tuesdays 10am PT = 7pm CET, should be OK.

Ava Chen: Good point. Let's propose Tue 10am PT / 7pm CET as the standing slot. Monthly workshop on the second Tue of the month, 90m. Agenda draft:```
- 0-10m: intros & success metrics check-in
- 10-40m: feature deep-dive or engineering clinic
- 40-60m: Q&A + action items for next week
- (monthly workshop adds 60-90m: hands-on lab + feedback capture)
```

Marcus Lee: Agenda looks good. I'll own the routing/fallbacks deep-dive (slide + 15m demo).

Riley Gomez: I'll prepare a short prompt engineering checklist + 2 live examples for the hands-on lab. Also can we capture a short pre-work survey to tailor the first workshop? Suggested questions: current usage patterns, top 3 pain points, top intents.

Priya Patel: I'll draft the customer invite and include recording + notes expectations. We should also add a renewal check-in at month 2 to surface any commercial risks.

Ava Chen: Nice. Let's add a pulse question into the office hours: "Is rollout on track?" — simple yes/needs help. We'll track answers in the account doc.

OnboardBot: NimbleHealth availability check: recurring slot Tue 10:00 PT is available for 10/12, 11/09, 12/14. Calendar invite template: https://redwood-internal/templates/enablement-invite.md

Marcus Lee: :thumbsup: I'll record my demo. Can someone add access to the staging tenant for NimbleHealth before 11/09?

Priya Patel: @Ava Chen I can request staging access today. Also will include the pre-work survey link in the invite.

Ava Chen: Thanks all — next steps: Priya sends invites + survey by EOD Wednesday, Marcus requests staging access, Riley and Marcus to upload slides to the shared enablement folder. We'll reconfirm one week before first session.

Riley Gomez: Got it. I'll drop the slides in /Shared/Enablement/NimbleHealth and ping when done.

Priya Patel: Sent invite draft to the customer (cc: marketing for recording permission). Will post final calendar once they confirm.

Ava Chen: Awesome. Closing the loop here — will mark this slot as tentatively booked in the account plan and follow up if customer proposes an alternative. :white_check_mark:

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — prepping the EBR snapshot for Meridian next Wed. Need slide owners, run order, and any open onboarding or renewal risks to call out. I put a draft deck here: https://redwood.cloud/drive/meridian_ebr_draft (please update directly).
Priya Rao: On it. I can own the onboarding status slide — will pull the latest health scores and NPS. Anyone with customer quotes?
Tom Ellis: I’ll take the 'product adoption & blockers' slide. Short bullets + 1 screenshot. ETA EOD tomorrow.
Marco Diaz: I’ll cover renewal posture and contract timeline. Quick note: finance flagged a potential discount ask — adding to risks.
Lena Park: I can do the enablement/readiness slide and capture training completions + outstanding enablement tasks. Need the champion list from CS ops.
Ava Chen: Thanks. Slide numbering in deck: 3 = onboarding, 5 = adoption, 7 = renewal, 9 = enablement. @Marco please add the forecasted ARR and the 'likely renewal' calc (conservative / base / upside).
Marco Diaz: Will add ARR bands. For the risk sentence, propose: "Customer adoption plateau + procurement timeline may increase churn risk to medium (20% carry)" — ok?
Priya Rao: That reads fine. I'll add a customer quote to slide 3: "API latency improved our search relevance" — flagged from CS notes.
Tom Ellis: Short blocker list for slide 5: 1) rate-limit tuning 2) model mismatch on long prompts 3) small report bug. Will annotate severity.
Ava Chen: Exec alignment — need 2 asks for the sponsor: 1) commit to a proof-of-concept for dedicated capacity (who owns ops cost?) 2) approval for a 30-day enablement cadence. Keep asks short, action-oriented.
Lena Park: I can craft the ask language. Also tagging training owners: @Marco to provide who from their team will join enablement.
Marco Diaz: I'll send names by EOD.
OpsBot: Deployment alert (prod): meridian/prod minor rollout completed — no errors reported. Logged at 1799987700.
Priya Rao: Quick check — do we want a canary metric on latency pre/post POC? I can add a small table.
Tom Ellis: Yes — latency p50/p95 and success rate. I’ll include baseline numbers.
Ava Chen: Great. Action items (short):
- Priya: update slide 3 with health scores + quote (due 1799990000)
- Tom: slide 5 blockers + baseline metrics (due 1799990000)
- Marco: renewal bands + attendee list for enablement (due 1799988200)
- Lena: write sponsor asks & enablement cadence (due 1799988200)
- All: review deck and comment inline (link above) by EOB day after tomorrow.
Priya Rao: Noted. Will flag any missing data in comments. :white_check_mark:
Marco Diaz: FYI I'll ping finance for discount guidance, might take 24h.
Lena Park: Heads-up, enablement ops needs one week lead time for custom materials.
Ava Chen: Thanks team. If no objections I’ll draft the exec readout script and share by tomorrow morning. :thumbsup:
Tom Ellis: SGTM
Priya Rao: SGTM
Marco Diaz: SGTM
Lena Park: SGTM
Ava Chen: End: will compile action tracker after updates and post here.

source.messages:
Priya Rao: Heads up — KestrelAI activity cratered this week, ~64% drop in DAUs. Their Head of Data (Marta) left Friday and procurement told our AE they're running a 2-week proof with CloudX. Need a quick risk play.
Tom Alvarez: Oof. do we have seat-level churn & which teams paused?
Priya Rao: short summary — 420 active seats last week -> 150 now. Top 3 product teams paused. Advocate was Marta (Head of Data). New contact is an interim PM, not responsive yet.
Evan Kim: drop the raw numbers and i'll pull infra logs to see if it's us (errors/timeouts) or client-side patterns.
Priya Rao: paste of daily active seats (last 7 days):
```
2026-03-14: 420
2026-03-15: 380
2026-03-16: 290
2026-03-17: 240
2026-03-18: 180
2026-03-19: 160
2026-03-20: 150
```
Priya Rao: also flagged by support — increased model response time for long prompts (timeouts) during 3/16–3/18 window. might've triggered the pilot.
Janelle Ortiz: noted. i saw 5 error SLO alerts for kestrel-prod in that window (e-4321, e-4322). i've linked support case #9876 to the account and added initial notes.
Ben Carter: propose short mitigation: 1) offer one-week free Dedicated slot to remove perf pain, 2) run a quick joint postmortem and proactive QBR with the interim PM, 3) prep a competitive brief vs CloudX highlighting latency/cost and model parity.
Tom Alvarez: +1. i'll own the CloudX pricing/latency comp. also recommend a canary rollback policy if we spin up Dedicated for them.
Evan Kim: i'll do perf root cause. if it's infra/kernel/runtime -> push hotfix asap. if it's user prompt patterns -> create enablement doc + quick prompt templates and a short co-run session.
Priya Rao: i'm reaching out to the interim PM now with proposed times for a 30m sync and offering the Dedicated pilot + a week of co-run enablement. will paste outreach copy in-thread.
Tom Alvarez: ping me the outreach copy and i'll review. also flag for AM exec escalation if no reply in 48h.
alert-bot: usage anomaly detected for account=kestrelai, drop_percent=64, started=2026-03-16T03:12:00Z
Priya Rao: capturing next steps: 1) Evan triage (perf vs config) due EOD, 2) Tom competitive brief due +1 day, 3) Priya outreach & Dedicated offer now, 4) Janelle to keep support case warm and surface any new errors. i'll schedule follow-up in #customer-success tomorrow.
Ben Carter: good plan. escalate to AM execs if no response within 48h.
Priya Rao: agreed — will escalate if no reply by 2026-03-22 noon PST.

text:
Priya Rao: Heads up — KestrelAI activity cratered this week, ~64% drop in DAUs. Their Head of Data (Marta) left Friday and procurement told our AE they're running a 2-week proof with CloudX. Need a quick risk play.
Tom Alvarez: Oof. do we have seat-level churn & which teams paused?
Priya Rao: short summary — 420 active seats last week -> 150 now. Top 3 product teams paused. Advocate was Marta (Head of Data). New contact is an interim PM, not responsive yet.
Evan Kim: drop the raw numbers and i'll pull infra logs to see if it's us (errors/timeouts) or client-side patterns.
Priya Rao: paste of daily active seats (last 7 days):
```
2026-03-14: 420
2026-03-15: 380
2026-03-16: 290
2026-03-17: 240
2026-03-18: 180
2026-03-19: 160
2026-03-20: 150
```
Priya Rao: also flagged by support — increased model response time for long prompts (timeouts) during 3/16–3/18 window. might've triggered the pilot.
Janelle Ortiz: noted. i saw 5 error SLO alerts for kestrel-prod in that window (e-4321, e-4322). i've linked support case #9876 to the account and added initial notes.
Ben Carter: propose short mitigation: 1) offer one-week free Dedicated slot to remove perf pain, 2) run a quick joint postmortem and proactive QBR with the interim PM, 3) prep a competitive brief vs CloudX highlighting latency/cost and model parity.
Tom Alvarez: +1. i'll own the CloudX pricing/latency comp. also recommend a canary rollback policy if we spin up Dedicated for them.
Evan Kim: i'll do perf root cause. if it's infra/kernel/runtime -> push hotfix asap. if it's user prompt patterns -> create enablement doc + quick prompt templates and a short co-run session.
Priya Rao: i'm reaching out to the interim PM now with proposed times for a 30m sync and offering the Dedicated pilot + a week of co-run enablement. will paste outreach copy in-thread.
Tom Alvarez: ping me the outreach copy and i'll review. also flag for AM exec escalation if no reply in 48h.

source.thread:
Aisha Patel: Quick sync — HelioMed asked for a hands-on migration + office hours the week of May 19. They want a 90m workshop + three 30m follow-ups. Who can lead the workshop? @Ben? :calendar:

Ben Carter: I can lead the 90m workshop. Prefer Thu 5/21 in the morning PT. Need a runbook + sample dataset to demo.

Priya Singh: I can take the 3x30m office hours the following week (Mon/Wed/Fri). If we lock the workshop time I’ll draft the office-hours agenda: 1) migration checklist 2) live migration demo 3) ticket triage + next steps.

Aisha Patel: Great. Jordan, can you create the invites (workshop + 3 office hours) and include an RSVP + prework doc? Also add "renewal risk check" as an agenda item so we capture concerns.

Jordan Lee: On it. I’ll provision a join link, add internal owners, and include a quick prework checklist. Will post the links here once done.

calendar-bot: Created event 'HelioMed — Migration Workshop (90m)' — Thu May 21 10:30–12:00 PT — https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=heliomed-workshop-5-21

Ben Carter: Runbook skeleton I’ll use (will paste into the shared doc):
```
- Pre-reqs (accounts, keys, sample data)
- Backup + rollback plan
- Migration steps (dry run, full run)
- Validation checks (latency, embedding checks)
- Post-migration monitoring
```
I’ll upload the sample dataset to the shared folder.

Aisha Patel: Please make sure to highlight data residency constraints and KV cache warmup time in the demo. Customer flagged those as top concerns.

Priya Singh: I’ll prepare a 3-question post-session survey to capture satisfaction + any renewal signals (deployment blockers, compliance gaps, budget timeline). Will share the survey link in the follow-ups.

Jordan Lee: Invites sent and guest list updated (HelioMed PMs + infra). Links: Workshop: https://meet.redwood.ai/heliomed-wrksp, OH1: https://meet.redwood.ai/heliomed-oh-1, OH2: https://meet.redwood.ai/heliomed-oh-2, OH3: https://meet.redwood.ai/heliomed-oh-3

Ben Carter: Nice. I added two dry-run slots for the demo — Wed 5/20 09:00 PT and Thu 5/21 08:00 PT. FYI for whoever wants to join.

Aisha Patel: Thanks team — logging this thread for the CS playbook and customer notes. I’ll capture action items: Ben runbook + sample data, Priya survey + office-hours agenda, Jordan invites + prework doc. :thumbsup:

Jordan Lee: All done on my side. Marking invites as confirmed.

Aisha Patel: Perfect. Closing here — will reopen if HelioMed requests changes.

source.text:
Ava Chen: Heads up — Meridian health review is next Tues (4/1). Need final slide deck + exec readouts. Quick sync: onboarding 78% complete, integration tests failing on webhook path, renewal currently 'watch' (C-level exec asked for usage numbers). Who can take slide ownership + run the dry run?

Diego Morales: I can own onboarding + tech blockers slide. Will collect webhook error counts and current ETA for fixes.

Priya Nair: I'll handle renewal risks + adoption metrics. Need last 90-day DAU/queries and top 5 customers using features. @Diego can you confirm instrumentation is up?

Samir Khan: I'll do the demo flow and script. Need a short version for execs (2 slides) and a backup fallback script if live demo fails. Draft by Mon EOD ok?

Elena Rodriguez: Capture all action items in the runbook (I will). Also can compile a one‑pager of ask/decision points for execs.

qbr-bot: Reminder: deck template v3 attached. Please upload final deck to /shared/qbrs/Meridian by 2026-03-30 17:00 UTC.

Ava Chen: Great. Action list:
- Diego: onboarding + webhook logs (owner) — due 2026-03-29
- Priya: renewal risk slide + usage metrics — due 2026-03-29
- Samir: demo script + backup — due 2026-03-30
- Elena: runbook + exec one-pager — due 2026-03-30
Add to calendar: dry run 2026-03-31 16:00 UTC — @all

Diego Morales: noted. I'll paste logs + short root-cause in thread by tomorrow.

Priya Nair: can someone grant me access to the customer GA dashboard? getting 403 today.

Ava Chen: @Elena can you add Priya to the dashboard? Also @Samir can you keep demo to 5 minutes? Execs hate long demos :smile:

Elena Rodriguez: Done — priya.nair@redwood added, confirmed. Also attached a tiny outline:
```
1) Exec ask & renewal status
2) Key adoption metrics (last 90d)
3) Onboarding blockers + ETA
4) Demo (2min highlight + fallback)
5) Decision asks & next steps
```

Ava Chen: Perfect — thanks team. Will compile final slide order after dry run.

source.text:
Ava Chen: quick heads-up — NovaThera flagged as AMBER in renewal dashboard after last exec call. They asked for a hands-on enablement sprint + office hours next week to unblock integration.
Mateo Ruiz: I can run the live inference demo and help with migration questions. what days are we thinking? :man_technologist:
Priya Desai: i'm available tues/thurs mornings (PST). can cover onboarding checklist + pre-reqs.
Lin Zhang: i can take the API walkthrough and ops-runbook section. also will capture gaps for the renewal playbook.
Ava Chen: thanks. proposal: 2-hr workshop (deep-dive + pairing on their infra) + two follow-up 45m office hours the next two days. trying to minimize disruption across APAC/EMEA. options below — vote with reaction or reply.
Ava Chen: Option A: Tue 15:00–17:00 UTC (Workshop) / Wed 14:00–14:45 UTC & Thu 14:00–14:45 UTC (Office hours)
Ava Chen: Option B: Thu 08:00–10:00 UTC (Workshop) / Fri 07:00–07:45 UTC & Mon 07:00–07:45 UTC (Office hours)
Mateo Ruiz: +1 Option A. better for demo latency from our EU infra.
Priya Desai: Option A works for me too. i'll prep the pre-read and quick token-cost calc.
Lin Zhang: i prefer Option B (early slot) — minimizes overlap with my oncall. can we split the workshop into 90 min demo + 30 min pairing rooms?
Ava Chen: yes — plan: 90m demo/architecture discussion, 30m breakout pairing rooms. agenda draft:
```agenda
- 00:00–00:10: welcome & goals
- 00:10–01:40: live demo + infra walkthrough (Mateo + Lin)
- 01:40–02:10: breakout pairing (customer-specific tasks)
- 02:10–02:15: quick commit & next steps
```
Calendar Bot: created provisional event: <https://calendar.redwood.com/event/novath-era-enable|NovaThera Enablement — provisional> (option placeholders)
Priya Desai: i'll upload prework doc + token budgeting sheet to the event and ping customer for required creds.
Mateo Ruiz: can someone bring the short runbook snippet for fallback models (quantized variant)? i can demo toggling in real time.
Lin Zhang: will do. also capturing 3 renewal risk signals during the workshop to add to the account playbook: latency spikes on large batches, auth timeouts on STS rotation, unclear SLO owners.
Ava Chen: perfect. volunteers: Mateo (demo), Lin (ops/runbook), Priya (prework + facilitator), me (customer success lead + renewal touch). if anyone else wants to join pairing rooms, add reaction :hand:
Priya Desai: quick ask — can we record workshop and ask NovaThera to complete a 3-question feedback survey immediately after? suggested survey: usefulness, blockers remaining, next support items.
Ava Chen: yes. recording + short survey. i'll add a Loom link to the wrap and set a 48h follow-up cadence.
Mateo Ruiz: fine by me. also will prepare a tiny `curl` snippet so they can replicate inference call locally:
```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -d '{"model":"rw-instruct-3b","input":"hello"}'
```
Lin Zhang: noted. i'll add a short troubleshooting doc in the event description.
Ava Chen: thanks team — final step: react with ✅ for Option A, 🚀 for Option B, or add a quick note if neither. i'll lock the slot end of day PST tomorrow and send the invite to NovaThera.
Calendar Bot: reminder set for 48h RSVP cutoff.
Ava Chen: tagging this to renewal playbook after the workshop so AMBER becomes GREEN if onboarding completes and action items done. :)

source.messages:
Alex Kim: quick sync — we need a short customer-facing RCA and timeline for ACME. They saw degraded embeddings for ~45m yesterday. Can we draft a safe summary and a follow-up plan? @Maria you own comms, @Devon please add what happened on call.
IncidentBot: Incident 2026-03-14-EMB-42 created by oncall_devon at 2026-03-14T02:40:12Z — severity: high. Paging: dev-oncall, eng-sre, customer-success.
Devon Patel: tl;dr — KV-cache corruption on a dedicated pool led to 5–25s tail latencies for embedding requests. Happened after a rolling kernel upgrade on the host fleet; one node failed to clear stale cache pointers so some inference workers served stale pointers -> OOM-ish behavior and long GC stalls. The autoscaler mitigated after 18m and we evicted the bad hosts manually.
Sophie Chen: adding details — kernel upgrade triggered a rare libnuma reorder. Repro locally with a stress runner and exact kernel + drm drivers. We rolled a hotfix that prevents the cache pointer reuse and forced a restart on affected hosts. Patch PR: https://git.internal/redwood/infra/commit/abc123
Devon Patel: timeline (local notes):
- 02:34 UTC: alert — embedding p95 > 2s, errors spike
- 02:36 UTC: oncall paged, started triage
- 02:45 UTC: identified hosts with repeated mem faults, isolated
- 02:58 UTC: autoscaler evicts/replicates; partial recovery starts
- 03:12 UTC: manual host drain and restart completed
- 03:19 UTC: monitoring shows latency back to baseline; closing incident
Maria Lopez: Draft customer message (short + non-technical):
"Hi ACME team — we want to follow up on the embedding degradation you experienced on 2026-03-14. Briefly: a software update on our host fleet caused a small subset of hosts to behave incorrectly, which slowed some embedding requests for about 45 minutes. We identified the issue, rolled a fix, and your workload recovered. There is no evidence of data loss. We will: 1) ensure the kernel update process includes this driver check, 2) add canary staging to that patch path, and 3) alert you if we see related anomalies. If you want a deeper technical RCA or a call, let us know and we can schedule. — Maria, Customer Success"
Ethan Park: ACME contact is OK with a short note — they asked for ETA on permanent mitigation and whether we can add a Dedicated health check for them.
Sophie Chen: Permanent mitigation plan (engineering phrasing for CS):
- Backport hotfix to kernel-update orchestration to skip nodes with the conflicting driver version.
- Add an automated preflight that runs a lightweight KV-cache integrity check after kernel change and before returning nodes to the pool.
- Add observable metric: kv_cache_pointer_reuse_count and an alert at >0 for 1m.
- Run a targeted canary (5% of capacity) for 24h on future host updates.
Devon Patel: Customer-impact assessment: Affected percentage of ACME requests ~3.8% during window; most requests were slightly delayed, <1% saw transient errors that retried successfully. No data exfiltration or model corruption.
Maria Lopez: Proposed status page update (short):
"We experienced a partial degradation of embedding latency for some customers on 2026-03-14 between 02:34–03:19 UTC due to a host-level software update. Services have recovered. Root cause: host kernel/driver interaction causing KV-cache instability. Remediation: host drains and a hotfix; longer-term mitigations in progress. For customers who saw impact, CS will follow up individually." — do we want to add the incident link?
Alex Kim: yes include the status link: https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/emb-2026-03-14 — keep wording non-jargony.
Maria Lopez: OK publishing now. I'll send the short customer email and CC engineering. Draft reply to ACME included above. Anyone want to tweak wording?
Sophie Chen: One small tweak: change "host kernel/driver interaction" -> "host software update interacting with a driver version" (less blame-y).
Maria Lopez: updated. Final customer email queued. Action items: @Sophie to open a ticket for the preflight check (ENG-1429), @Devon to add the metric + alert (OBS-560), @Alex to confirm follow-up call if ACME requests.
Devon Patel: logging snippet from the incident for internal RCA:
```
[02:35:19] worker-7 ERROR kv_cache.cpp: pointer reuse detected, refcnt mismatch
[02:35:20] worker-7 WARN memory_manager.cpp: attempt to serve with stale pointer -> paused
[02:36:02] autoscaler INFO evicting host i-0a12bc34 (oom-like symptoms)
```
Maria Lopez: ACME replied: "Thanks for the quick follow-up. We appreciate the clarity. Please schedule a call next week to review the mitigation timeline." Forwarding to @Alex and @Devon for scheduling. :thumbsup:
Alex Kim: I'll set up a 30m call Wednesday 15:00 UTC — invite in calendar. Closing internal thread once the customer call is scheduled and ENG tickets created.
DeployBot: Rollback job 9876 completed at 2026-03-14T03:05:01Z — affected hosts drained.
Devon Patel: noted. I'll open ENG-1429 and OBS-560 now and paste back links when created.
Alex Kim: thanks all — good execution on triage. Maria, please paste final sent email into this thread for records after send.
Maria Lopez: done — pasted the exact sent email below for audit: "Hi ACME team — we followed up on the embedding degradation from 2026-03-14... [sent]."
DeployBot: Incident 2026-03-14-EMB-42 closed by dev-oncall at 2026-03-14T03:22:11Z.

- 02:58 UTC: autoscaler evicts/replicates; partial recovery starts
- 03:12 UTC: manual host drain and restart completed
- 03:19 UTC: monitoring shows latency back to baseline; closing incident
Maria Lopez: Draft customer message (short + non-technical):
"Hi ACME team — we want to follow up on the embedding degradation you experienced on 2026-03-14. Briefly: a software update on our host fleet caused a small subset of hosts to behave incorrectly, which slowed some embedding requests for about 45 minutes. We identified the issue, rolled a fix, and your workload recovered. There is no evidence of data loss. We will: 1) ensure the kernel update process includes this driver check, 2) add canary staging to that patch path, and 3) alert you if we see related anomalies. If you want a deeper technical RCA or a call, let us know and we can schedule. — Maria, Customer Success"
Ethan Park: ACME contact is OK with a short note — they asked for ETA on permanent mitigation and whether we can add a Dedicated health check for them.
Sophie Chen: Permanent mitigation plan (engineering phrasing for CS):
- Backport hotfix to kernel-update orchestration to skip nodes with the conflicting driver version.
- Add an automated preflight that runs a lightweight KV-cache integrity check after kernel change and before returning nodes to the pool.
- Add observable metric: kv_cache_pointer_reuse_count and an alert at >0 for 1m.
- Run a targeted canary (5% of capacity) for 24h on future host updates.
Devon Patel: Customer-impact assessment: Affected percentage of ACME requests ~3.8% during window; most requests were slightly delayed, <1% saw transient errors that retried successfully. No data exfiltration or model corruption.
Maria Lopez: Proposed status page update (short):
"We experienced a partial degradation of embedding latency for some customers on 2026-03-14 between 02:34–03:19 UTC due to a host-level software update. Services have recovered. Root cause: host kernel/driver interaction causing KV-cache instability. Remediation: host drains and a hotfix; longer-term mitigations in progress. For customers who saw impact, CS will follow up individually." — do we want to add the incident link?
Alex Kim: yes include the status link: https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/emb-2026-03-14 — keep wording non-jargony.
Maria Lopez: OK publishing now. I'll send the short customer email and CC engineering. Draft reply to ACME included above. Anyone want to tweak wording?
Sophie Chen: One small tweak: change "host kernel/driver interaction" -> "host software update interacting with a driver version" (less blame-y).
Maria Lopez: updated. Final customer email queued. Action items: @Sophie to open a ticket for the preflight check (ENG-1429), @Devon to add the metric + alert (OBS-560), @Alex to confirm follow-up call if ACME requests.
Devon Patel: logging snippet from the incident for internal RCA:
```
[02:35:19] worker-7 ERROR kv_cache.cpp: pointer reuse detected, refcnt mismatch
[02:35:20] worker-7 WARN memory_manager.cpp: attempt to serve with stale pointer -> paused
[02:36:02] autoscaler INFO evicting host i-0a12bc34 (oom-like symptoms)
```
Maria Lopez: ACME replied: "Thanks for the quick follow-up. We appreciate the clarity. Please schedule a call next week to review the mitigation timeline." Forwarding to @Alex and @Devon for scheduling. :thumbsup:
Alex Kim: I'll set up a 30m call Wednesday 15:00 UTC — invite in calendar. Closing internal thread once the customer call is scheduled and ENG tickets created.

source.thread:
Maya Chen: quick flag — QuantaSafe onboarding: p95 tail ~2.05s on prod, token spend ~2.4x vs forecast. Renewal in ~32 days, marking as medium9risk. Proposing a short proactive pilot: mixed 4-bit quant profile (quant4) + sample-based routing of long requests to their dedicated pool. Need eng + SRE + enablement to approve a 48h canary.

Ethan Park: can you drop the perf dash and a representative trace?

Maya Chen: dash: https://redwood.app/d/quantasafe/ops?panelId=tailing
trace: `req=qs-212 seq=1..768 p50=95ms p95=2.05s model=redwood/merlin-13b`

Ethan Park: initial plan: try "merlin-13b-quant4" (4-bit for attention & some dense ops, int8 fallback) + route seq_len>512 requests with sample_rate=0.12 to dedicated-quantasafe-us-east for canary. Expect ~35-40% token-eq unit cost reduction and lower scheduling backlog. Quick canary snippet below.

Ethan Park: ```
{"selector": "customer_id == \"quantasafe\" && random() < 0.12",
 "model": "redwood/merlin-13b-quant4",
 "pool": "dedicated-quantasafe-us-east",
 "route": {"condition": "seq_len > 512", "sample_rate": 0.12},
 "prefix_cache": {"enabled": true, "ttl_minutes": 45},
 "max_concurrency": 60,
 "fallback": "redwood/merlin-13b",
 "monitoring": {"slo_p95_ms": 1300, "error_budget_pct": 0.5}
}
```

Lia Wong: I can run a 45-min enablement: 10m infra walkthrough, 20m canary ops, 15m customer playbook + renewal talking points. Available Tue/Thu next week.

Samira Patel: nice. Can we get a rough cost delta to include in renewal notes (conservative vs optimistic)?

Ethan Park: rough back-of-envelope: baseline unit ~$0.019/token-eq. quant4 path ~37% lower => ~$0.012/l. For QuantaSafe ~2.4B tokens/mo that is ~ $31k/mo optimistic, conservative assume half until validated. We'll refine after canary.

AutoAlerts Bot: [metric alert] quantasafe_prod_avg_seq_len +20% 24h; token_rate +15%. Created ticket CS-5120.

Maya Chen: linking CS-5120 so SRE/eng can correlate infra logs. @Jules SRE  can you confirm pool headroom and reserve a slot for 48h?

Jules SRE: I can reserve a 1-GPU slot on dedicated-quantasafe-us-east for 48h. Need IAM list and expected peak qps. Will also attach temporary telemetry relabeling.

Maya Chen: expected steady qps ~140, spikes ~360. IAM: maya@redwood, ethan@redwood, lia@redwood. I'll draft a short customer-facing note with pilot goals, rollback criteria, and the cost estimate for review before sending.

Samira Patel: please include a one-paragraph executive summary for the renewal packet showing p95 improvement target, estimated monthly savings, and rollback triggers. Loop me in on the review.

Maya Chen: will do 9thanks team :thumbsup:
source._description: Proactive CS pilot for QuantaSafe: propose 4-bit mixed quant model swap + sample-based routing of long requests to a dedicated pool for a 48h canary; coordinate eng, SRE, enablement; capture cost delta and renewal talking points

source.messages:
Diego Morales: flag from renewal check-ins: three customers mentioned the same UX friction around model selection. feels like a cross-account signal. wondering if we should standardize how we surface multi-account recurring asks.
Leah Chen: agreed. PMs miss the pattern when items are scattered. what if CS created a tiny "Lighthouse Card" we can pin to the top of the notes so PMs see the pattern immediately?
Priya Singh: like a lighthouse that highlights repeated pain across customers? i like the metaphor. can you propose a compact template?
Diego Morales: 2-line template suggestion below 5 paste into notes or thread reply.
```
Lighthouse Card
- Issue (1 short sentence) + sample account count
- Business effect (renewal risk / onboarding delay / revenue impact)
- Suggested short-term workaround
- Priority: P0/P1/P2
- Tag(s): onboarding | renewal-risk | integrations | ux
```
Ben Carter: can we add a tiny field: "pattern evidence" (list of 1-2 lines with customer names + quote) so PMs can see it's cross-account without digging? keeps it honest.
Diego Morales: good call. updated sample with evidence line and an example filled card.
```
Lighthouse Card
- Issue: model selection defaults hide custom models (3 accounts reported)
- Business effect: blocks onboarding / renewal risk for two pilots (~$10k ARR risk)
- Workaround: enable "show all" toggle in UI for affected orgs
- Pattern evidence: AcmeRetail: "we can't find model X"; NovaHealth: "defaults hide our model"
- Priority: P1
- Tags: onboarding | renewal-risk | ux
```
Leah Chen: love the example. can ProductOps give us a shared sheet / export so cards roll into product triage automatically?
ProductOps Bot: Created shared template: https://go.redwood.ai/cs/lighthouse-cards (editable). Rows labeled "renewal-risk" auto-export to triage board on Fridays. :robot_face:
Priya Singh: pilot plan: 2-week pilot across onboarding + renewal outreach. target: 15 cards, at least 5 with cross-account evidence. any P0 -> immediate PM escalation.
Diego Morales: i'll add "create 1 Lighthouse Card" to the renewal call checklist for this week and try to pull evidence into the sheet.
Ben Carter: RenewalBot also flagged three accounts with similar NPS comments this morning. @RenewalBot can you attach the NPS snippets to the sheet?
RenewalBot: NPS snippets for flagged accounts uploaded to the Lighthouse sheet. Recommended converts: 3 snippets -> 2 cards.
Leah Chen: great. acceptance criteria for pilot: 1) 15 cards captured in 2 weeks, 2) at least 5 show cross-account pattern evidence, 3) PM triage receives export weekly and creates at least 1 follow-up ticket if priority >= P1.
Priya Singh: i'll run a short retro at the end of pilot and recommend next steps (embed card macro in Confluence / or add to onboarding playbook).
Diego Morales: thanks team. small structure, big signal. i'll post first cards later today from AcmeRetail call.
Leah Chen: excited to see if this reduces churn surprises. :sparkles:

source.content:
Jasmine Liu: Need quick help for exec escalation email draft going out in ~45 min. Can someone from SRE give us 2-3 sentences of a *customer-safe* mitigation statement for the streaming disconnects? (No internal hostnames, no vendor blame, no “we think”.)

Rachel Kim: +1. Also need confirmation on whether we’re calling this “resolved” vs “mitigated / monitoring”. Customer asked for: what happened, what we did, what to do if it recurs.

Dev Patel: Context dump (sanitized):
- Impact: elevated streaming disconnects/reset mid-response for subset of requests in us-east
- Start: ~18:05 UTC
- Peak: ~18:20–18:45 UTC (tickets + elevated error %)
- Current: back to normal range as of ~19:10 UTC, but we’re watching
- Customer-facing symptoms: stream resets, truncated output, client retries sometimes succeed
- Workaround we’ve been sharing: retry w/ exponential backoff + increase client timeouts; optionally disable HTTP/2 if behind certain proxies

Owen Phillips: For the email, we should align w/ the comms template (“ack / mitigation / next update”). Also do we want to mention fallback routing was activated briefly?

Sean Gallagher: SRE view: we should keep it crisp. Something like “we identified and mitigated an issue affecting streaming connections in us-east; applied config change; monitoring; next steps underway.” Rafael can confirm the exact phrasing.

Rafael Mendes: Yep. Proposed customer-safe statement:
“Between ~18:05–19:10 UTC we saw elevated streaming connection resets for some requests in the us-east region. We implemented a mitigation to stabilize long-lived streaming connections and error rates returned to normal levels. We’re continuing to monitor closely and are working on a permanent fix to reduce the likelihood of recurrence.”

Rachel Kim: This is good. Can we add 1 line on what *they* should do if it happens again (and what to send Support)?

Rafael Mendes: Add-on (still customer-safe):
“If you see a reset again, a retry with backoff should succeed. Please include request IDs and timestamps (UTC) in your reply so we can correlate quickly.”

Dev Patel: Perfect. Also checking: are we comfortable saying “permanent fix in progress” without an ETA? 

Sean Gallagher: Yes, as long as no date/ETA. Avoid “won’t happen again.”

Owen Phillips: Re fallback mention: I’d avoid unless they asked directly. If we mention it, it opens “did you ignore our pin?” questions.

Jasmine Liu: Agree, keep it tight. Rachel, can you drop this into the exec escalation template and send for final review?

Rachel Kim: Will do. Also, status page: we didn’t post, correct? If asked, we’ll say metrics didn’t meet threshold for a public incident, but we’re documenting internally.

Rafael Mendes: Correct — didn’t cross the status-page criteria. If needed, you can say “we investigated immediately and mitigated; we’ll follow up if we see renewed impact.”

Naomi Feldman: Quick reminder: don’t include any customer-specific architecture details (proxy/CDN names, network paths). Workaround wording should stay generic (“some client/proxy configurations”).

Dev Patel: Got it. I’ll update Support ticket notes w/ the approved phrasing so we stay consistent.

Jasmine Liu: Thanks all. :thumbsup: Sending in ~15.

source.thread:
Rachel Kim: QBR question for the known-issues sprint work—how are we messaging *temporary workarounds* in QBR decks + follow-up emails?

Rachel Kim: Specifically: what’s ok to document as a general workaround (KB) vs what should stay customer-specific (CS plan / account notes)? I’m worried we over-share “internal toggles” or create expectations that it’s a product feature.

Jasmine Liu: +1. Also want consistent language across CSMs so we don’t get “you promised X in QBR” escalations later.

Priya Shah: From Support side: if we put it in the KB, it needs to be copy/paste-ready + safe. If it’s “ask Redwood to flip a flag” that’s not a workaround—that’s an escalation path.

Elliot Price: Docs POV: propose a simple classification:
1) **Public-ish (Help Center / docs site):** workaround is safe + general + doesn’t expose internals. Includes clear “Temporary” label + “When to escalate”.
2) **Internal Confluence (Support/CS only):** operational guidance, internal dashboards, on-call routing, non-customer-safe details.
3) **Account-specific guidance (email / CS plan):** anything that depends on customer topology, contract terms, or Redwood-side tuning that we don’t want discoverable.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE: +1 and I’d add an explicit rule: anything that could be interpreted as a capacity/architecture admission should not go in a QBR deck verbatim.

Sean Gallagher: Example of what *not* to say: “we had to reroute you due to regional GPU pressure”. Better: “we observed elevated error rates in <region> and applied mitigation; monitoring for recurrence.”

Naomi Feldman: Security/compliance lens: QBR decks sometimes get forwarded widely inside customer orgs. Please avoid:
- internal component names / cluster IDs
- customer-specific logs / request IDs
- configuration screenshots from Console that reveal other tenants/regions

Naomi Feldman: If we need to share evidence, do it in a targeted email thread, sanitized, and confirm distribution list.

Talia Benson: Can we turn this into a reusable QBR slide + talk track? Like “Known Issues & Mitigations (Last 90 days)” with standard footers.

Wei Chen: I can draft language + a template slide. Suggest these fields:
- Symptom (customer-facing)
- Impact (customer-facing)
- Mitigation/workaround (customer-actionable)
- Status (temporary / fix in progress / fixed)
- ETA (only if confirmed)
- Escalation trigger (when to contact Support)

Rachel Kim: Yes. My biggest edge case: sometimes we recommend a workaround that’s “safe-ish” but not universally true (e.g., client-side streaming settings). Do we publish those?

Priya Shah: For stuff like streaming timeouts/reconnect: we can publish **recommended client settings** and call out “validate in staging” + “if you’re behind a proxy, coordinate w/ your network team.” That’s better than keeping it tribal.

Sean Gallagher: Agree, as long as it’s framed as “mitigate intermittency” not “fixes the issue.” Also include “if this increases error rate / duplicates, stop and escalate.”

Elliot Price: Proposal for the decision boundary:
- If the workaround is **customer-controlled** (SDK retry/backoff, timeout tuning, request id logging) and we have SRE/Eng signoff → publish in KB.
- If it requires **Redwood-side per-account configuration** (routing policy overrides, capacity pinning exceptions, bespoke limits) → keep customer-specific and document internally.

Jasmine Liu: +1. Also: QBR decks should not include step-by-step runbooks. They should link to the KB article (or a short appendix) so we can update the guidance without reissuing the deck.

Rachel Kim: Love that. For QBR: we can say “Current workaround: <one sentence>” + “See KB for latest steps.”

Wei Chen: Suggested standard disclaimer (QBR + email):
“Workarounds are provided as temporary mitigations and may change as we roll out fixes. We’ll notify you if guidance updates.”

Naomi Feldman: Please add: “Do not share externally” is not enforceable, but we can label as “Customer Confidential” if it contains account details. Otherwise keep it generic.

Priya Shah: For the KB itself, can we add a consistent callout:
- “Temporary workaround”
- “What to send Support” (region, model, request IDs, SDK ver)
- “When to escalate” (e.g., >X% failures, multiple regions, no mitigation)

Sean Gallagher: +1. That last piece aligns with the new escalation triggers.

Talia Benson: Action items?

Elliot Price: I’ll add a short section to the docs publishing checklist: “Is this safe for QBR/customer-wide distribution?” + pick one of the 3 classifications.

Wei Chen: I’ll draft the QBR slide/talk track template and share in this channel for review (Rachel/Jasmine/Naomi).

Rachel Kim: I’ll update the CSM QBR guidance: “link to KB, one-sentence workaround summary only, no internal details, no unconfirmed ETAs.”

Jasmine Liu: Great. Let’s also remind folks: if it’s a Redwood-side action, treat it as an escalation path, not a workaround.

Priya Shah: :thumbsup: I’ll align Support macros to match the same language (temporary / validate / when to escalate).

Naomi Feldman: :thumbsup: ping me before publishing anything that mentions security controls, audit logs, or private deployment internals.

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup: and ping SRE for signoff on anything that touches incident mitigation language or could conflict w/ status page comms.

Rachel Kim: Perfect. Thanks all.

Rachel Kim: Pulling together the “repeat questions” we keep getting from enterprise accounts. Goal = feed the known-issues sprint shortlist + make sure the KB answers are copy/paste ready. Please drop:
1) the question as the customer asks it
2) tier (Hosted/Dedicated/Private)
3) what artifact we need (KB, macro, dashboard link, runbook excerpt)

Rachel Kim: Starting w/ the top 3 from my side:
- “Are streaming disconnects on you or on us? What should we set our client timeouts to?” (Hosted)
- “We’re getting 429s but the headers say we have quota left—what do these headers actually mean?” (Hosted)
- “Audit logs are delayed—what’s the expected delivery SLO and how do we validate it?” (Enterprise/Private-ish depending)

Jasmine Liu: +1. Also please flag anything where we’re currently over-sharing in email (customer-specific infra details) so Docs can generalize it.

Priya Shah: Repeat Q I see constantly:
- “What info do you need to debug this?” -> request IDs + region + model + SDK version + timeframe. Customers ask because we request it inconsistently.
Artifact: updated macro + intake checklist snippet for CSMs to paste.

Dev Patel: :eyes: yes pls. If CS is sending asks, it needs to match the Support intake checklist. I can share a mini-template we can all use.

Talia Benson: Adding Dedicated customer repeats (esp post-incident):
- “Why did latency spike if the cluster was ‘healthy’?” (Dedicated)
- “Is autoscaling safe to leave on? How do we prevent scale up/down thrash?” (Dedicated)
Artifact: KB + “when to escalate” + config knobs (min replicas, cooldowns) + link to dashboard.

Chris Osei: From metrics review: tickets cluster around 5 themes we can deflect w/ docs:
1) streaming timeouts/reconnect
2) quota/429/rate-limit headers
3) dedicated autoscaling oscillation
4) private upgrade stuck at migrations
5) audit log delivery lag
If folks agree, I’ll map each to (ticket volume, avg time-to-mitigation, tier) in the triage sheet.

Elliot Price: For Docs: please include the *exact phrasing* customers use. We can turn those into H2s / FAQ callouts. Also confirm if these should live under “Known issues” vs “Troubleshooting” (we’ve been mixing).

Wei Chen: Hosted repeats:
- “Streaming response ended early / truncated mid-token. Is that a token limit?” (Usually proxy/timeout/reconnect confusion)
- “If we retry a streaming request do we get double-billed?” (billing anxiety + idempotency question)
Artifacts: KB + SDK troubleshooting section + macro.

Kira Thompson: Private repeats:
- “Installer upgrade stuck at migrations—can we safely roll back?”
- “What logs are safe to send? We can’t share pod logs w/ PHI.”
Artifacts: KB (w/ explicit ‘safe artifacts’ list) + escalation path to Private Deployments.

Owen Phillips: Adding the “where do I go” confusion:
- “Do we open a SUP ticket or ping Slack? When does this become an incident?”
Not customer-facing, but internal CS/Support alignment. Artifact: quick reference + bridge template.

Rachel Kim: Another one from exec stakeholders:
- “Should you post a status page update?” / “Why wasn’t this on the status page?”
We need consistent language for: status page vs docs banner vs direct email.

Jasmine Liu: +1. That one burns a lot of trust.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE: repeat question from enterprise postmortems:
- “If this happens again, what will you do in the first 15 minutes?”
We should have a generic answer (triage steps + comms cadence) that does NOT promise specific technical actions.
Artifact: comms template snippet + runbook excerpt w/ redactions.

Dev Patel: Proposing we turn these into 5 “Known issue” articles + 5 “Support macros” + 1 “comms alignment” link that CS can always include.
Known-issues candidates (draft titles only for us):
- streaming timeouts/reconnect
- 429/quota mismatch
- dedicated autoscaling oscillation
- private upgrade stuck
- audit log delivery latency

Priya Shah: For the 429 one, customers also ask “why is limit different for chat vs embeddings?” We should explain that headers may be per-route + rolling window.

Chris Osei: Capturing in sheet. For each, I’ll add: severity risk + escalations involved.

Elliot Price: Re: placement: I’d suggest Known Issues hub as the landing, and each article includes:
- Symptoms
- Impact
- Affected (models/regions/tiers)
- Workaround (copy/paste)
- When to escalate
- Last updated + owner

Wei Chen: On streaming: we should explicitly call out safe client behaviors:
- exponential backoff retry
- shorter server read timeout than load balancer idle timeout
- log request IDs
- don’t assume truncation == max_tokens

Kira Thompson: On private upgrades: we need to be careful w/ rollback steps. “Safe if you haven’t run X migration” etc. We should keep it as “supported rollback path” not “do whatever.”

Jasmine Liu: Agree. Anything risky should say “coordinate w/ Support/on-call” and be marked temporary.

Rachel Kim: Assigning owners? Rough:
- Streaming article: Wei + Dev review
- 429/quota: Priya Shah + Platform review (Ethan?)
- Dedicated autoscaling: Talia + SRE review
- Private upgrade: Kira + Platform/Private Deployments review
- Audit log lag: Rachel + SRE review

Dev Patel: Works. Also we should add “request ID + timeframe” guidance to every one. Customers LOVE concrete asks.

Owen Phillips: +1 and please add “what to include when escalating” box in each article. Otherwise we still get “it’s broken” tickets.

Chris Osei: I’ll add a column for “required diagnostic fields” so the macro and article stay consistent.

Elliot Price: Quick reminder: no customer names, no account IDs, no screenshots with customer data. If you need an example, use sanitized/fictional payloads.

Priya Shah: :thumbsup: I can also refresh the macro snippets to always end with:
`Region:`
`Model:`
`SDK version:`
`Request IDs (2-3):`
`Time range (UTC):`
`Any recent deploys/changes:`

Rachel Kim: Love it. Also for enterprise, add “Is this production impact?” because they sometimes report as “prod down” when it’s a single job.

Sean Gallagher: One more repeat question I’ve heard: “You said you have fallback—does that mean you’ll ignore our pinned model version?”
That’s a big trust issue. If we don’t have a public answer yet, at least an internal macro + dashboard link for Support/CS.

Dev Patel: Yep, that maps to the routing/fallback confusion ticket cluster. Might be a phase 2 article if we can validate the exact behavior w/ Platform.

Jasmine Liu: Let’s keep sprint scope tight to the top 5. But we should log that as next-up.

Rachel Kim: Action items:
- Chris: update triage sheet w/ these questions + rough counts
- Elliot: confirm KB template + where hub lives
- Owners: drop 1-2 customer-phrased questions under each topic by EOD

Talia Benson: Adding 2 literal quotes from Northstar:
- “Why did autoscaling add GPUs and still get slower?”
- “Can we cap scale-down so we don’t thrash during peak?”

Wei Chen: Literal quote from Acme:
- “We can’t keep an SSE stream open for 2 minutes through our proxy. What’s the recommended timeout + reconnect behavior?”

Kira Thompson: Literal quote from Helix:
- “If the upgrade is stuck, can we roll back without corrupting data? What exactly do you need from us if we can’t ship logs?”

Priya Shah: Literal quote from Aurora:
- “Your docs say we have 60 RPM but the API says 10. Which is real?”

Elliot Price: Perfect. I’ll turn these into FAQ headers.

Dev Patel: I’ll mirror the “required fields” template into Support intake + the new macros doc so CS/Support are aligned.

Jasmine Liu: Thanks all. This is exactly the kind of stuff that reduces escalations. Once we publish, we’ll also link from the Support workflows so it’s hard to miss.

source.messages:
Sofia Li: Putting together the Cedar Health onboarding internal prep. Need quick roll call and who will present what. Internal sync tomorrow 10:30am PT?
Noah Brooks: +1 for 10:30. I can cover infra, perf expectations, and any capacity plumbing.
Priya Shah: I'll own success criteria and renewal watch. Quick note: Cedar's procurement window closes ~2026-06-15, so renewal risk if no production signal by day 45.
Ben Carter: I'll do quickstart + sample code. Suggest we highlight webhook consumer and python snippet in the enablement.
Sofia Li: Stakeholders to flag: Rajesh (Cedar PM) - primary stake; Marcus (security) - reviewer; Priya (Cedar data lead). Internal owners: Sofia (CS Lead), Noah (SRE/infra), Ben (SE), Priya (CS Ops).
Noah Brooks: Proposed tech timeline: Day 0 - creds & staging env; Day 1-5 - integration & schema mapping; Day 6-14 - validation + perf tuning; Day 15-30 - pilot + early roll. If they request private/VPC we need +7 days for security review.
Priya Shah: 30-day success metrics (short): 1) Staging app makes authenticated API calls; 2) >=80% of sample prompts meet quality gate; 3) Health dashboard live with alerts; 4) Rajesh confirms acceptance email.
Ben Carter: Quick code sample for the enablement slide deck to show devs a minimal consumer:
```python
# minimal consumer example
import requests
resp = requests.post('https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate', json={'model':'rwd-m','prompt':'Summarize notes'})
print(resp.json())
```
Sofia Li: Risks: procurement timeline slipping, security gating private deploy, embedding scale if they batch heavily. Renewal signal: no value in first 45 days -> high risk. Action: schedule sec touch on Day 2.
oncall-bot: Security review slot reserved for Cedar Health on 2026-04-02 14:00 UTC.
Noah Brooks: Capacity note: if Cedar expects >500 embeddings req/min recommend a short Dedicated trial or aggressive batching. Tagging @infra-sales for pricing follow-up.
Priya Shah: Action items: Ben - deck + quickstart; Noah - perf checklist + infra notes; Sofia - customer invite + pre-read; me - compile success board and post-demo survey (3 Qs). I'll capture feedback and surface renewal flags.
Sofia Li: I will run an internal dry run 30 min before customer call and then invite Rajesh and Marcus. Please add 5 example prompts to the doc: https://redwood.internal/cs/cedar-prompts (placeholder).
Ben Carter: Uploaded quickstart and "Gotchas" slide to the folder.
Sofia Li: Thanks all. Closing internal prep thread; will post customer invite link after our rehearsal. :rocket:

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick heads-up — Im pulling together an account-health + budget readout for Atlasicas renewal meeting next Thu. Need: token burn snapshot (30/60/90d), activation funnel impact, and any onboarding blockers that tie to cost. @Priya can you grab the latest spend forecast?

Priya Singh: On it. Ill pull the 30/60/90 windows and a run-rate projection. Do we want a blended forecast (hosted + dedicated) or just hosted?

Ava Chen: Blended please — their usage is Hosted with a Dedicated pilot for embeddings. Also include any spikes from bulk-embed jobs.

Diego Ramos: FYI we flagged a surge last month from their nightly batch. I can add a short note on mitigation: suggested `concurrency cap = 8` + `batching = 512` for embeddings.

Marcus Lee: Onboarding blocker: SSO rollout delayed ingest of ~40% of new seats; that suppressed per-user token discount assumptions. Ill summarize in 1-2 bullets.

Priya Singh: Draft numbers for eyeballs:
```
30d tokens: 48M
60d tokens: 86M
90d tokens: 132M
Current run-rate: ~1.46M tokens/day
Forecast (next 90d baseline): 140-180M tokens
Cost impact: +12-18% vs last quarter
```

Ava Chen: Can you also produce conservative/medium/upside scenarios and call out assumptions (e.g., embed pilot converts 10/25/50% of queries)?

Priya Singh: Will do. Conservative = no conversion; medium = 25%; upside = 50%.

Diego Ramos: Risk: nightly large-context job accidentally ran with 4k token prompts twice last week — ~30% single-day spike. Recommend alert on avg tokens/request > 1.2k and a temporary quota guard.

Ava Chen: Marcus, can you confirm onboarding docs mention model/seq-length best-practices?

Marcus Lee: Not yet. Ill update the checklist and add a link in the readout.

cs-bot: Reminder — QBR cost template updated: https://redwood.company/qbr-templates (use `budget-risk` section).

Priya Singh: Deck plan: 1) current health + flags, 2) spend scenarios + assumptions, 3) mitigations + enablement asks.

Ava Chen: Enablement asks: 1) training on embed batching, 2) walkthrough of quota guard config, 3) one-off credit for spike (TBD). Who schedules the training? @Diego?

Diego Ramos: I can run batching session. Propose Wed morning.

Marcus Lee: Ill own quota-guard walkthrough and update onboarding docs.

Priya Singh: Ill recommend a credit range in the executive summary. Also flagging renewal risk: projected spend variance > 15% could trigger procurement pushback.

Diego Ramos: Testing small throttle in staging today — will paste logs if odd.

Priya Singh: Uploaded draft: https://drive.redwood/atlasica/qbr-draft — comments welcome.

Marcus Lee: Commented on slide 2 re: assumptions.

Diego Ramos: Staging throttle test: latency +5ms, throughput -2%, token-cost down 14% for batch jobs.

Ava Chen: Final exec summary should call out 3 quick wins: throttle/batching config, onboarding doc + SSO timeline, contingency credit ask.

Priya Singh: Final deck due Tue 10am PT.

Ava Chen: Closing thread — will ping for anything else. :wave:
